Original: Static defaults are the best choice for values which are logically required, but which have a value that works well for most users.  Static defaulting must not consider any state except the object being operated upon (and the complexity of Service API stands as an example of why).
Constraint(s): Static defaulting must not consider any state except the object being operated upon (and the complexity of Service API stands as an example of why).
Interpretation: 静的デフォルトは操作対象のオブジェクト以外の状態を考慮してはならない．

Original: The name of a list kind must end with "List". Lists have a limited set of common metadata. All lists use the required "items" field to contain the array of objects they return. Any kind that has the "items" field must be a list kind.
Constraint(s): Any kind that has the "items" field must be a list kind.
Interpretation: 「items」フィールドを持つkindは必ずリスト型でなければならない．非リスト型にitemsフィールドを定義してはならない．

Original: - They _typically_ do not use pointer types in the Go definition (e.g. `AnotherFlag SomeFlag`), though required fields where the zero value is a valid value must use pointer types, paired with an `omitempty` struct tag to avoid spurious null serializations.
Constraint(s): They _typically_ do not use pointer types in the Go definition (e.g. `AnotherFlag SomeFlag`), though required fields where the zero value is a valid value must use pointer types, paired with an `omitempty` struct tag to avoid spurious null serializations.
Interpretation: ゼロ値が有効な値であるrequiredフィールドは，ポインタ型を使用し，omitempty構造体タグと組み合わせて偽のnullシリアライゼーションを避けなければならない．

Original: * Condition type names should describe the current observed state of the resource, rather than describing the current state transitions. This typically means that the name should be an adjective ("Ready", "OutOfDisk") or a past-tense verb ("Succeeded", "Failed") rather than a present-tense verb ("Deploying"). Intermediate states may be indicated by setting the `status` of the condition to `Unknown`.
Constraint(s): Condition type names should describe the current observed state of the resource, rather than describing the current state transitions. This typically means that the name should be an adjective ("Ready", "OutOfDisk") or a past-tense verb ("Succeeded", "Failed") rather than a present-tense verb ("Deploying"). Intermediate states may be indicated by setting the `status` of the condition to `Unknown`.
Interpretation: Condition type名は現在の観測状態を表すべきであり，状態遷移そのものを表すべきではない．形容詞（Ready）や過去形動詞（Succeeded）が望ましく，現在形動詞（Deploying）は避ける．

Original: Controllers should be authored with the assumption that the referenced resource may not exist, and include error handling to make the issue clear to the user.
Constraint(s): Controllers should be authored with the assumption that the referenced resource may not exist, and include error handling to make the issue clear to the user.
Interpretation: コントローラーは参照先リソースが存在しない可能性を前提として作成し，ユーザーに問題を明確に伝えるエラーハンドリングを含めるべきである．

Original: Some fields will have a list of allowed values (enumerations). These values will be strings, and they will be in CamelCase, with an initial uppercase letter. Examples: `ClusterFirst`, `Pending`, `ClientIP`. When an acronym or initialism each letter in the acronym should be uppercase, such as with `ClientIP` or `TCPDelay`. When a proper name or the name of a command-line executable is used as a constant the proper name should be represented in consistent casing - examples: `systemd`, `iptables`, `IPVS`, `cgroupfs`, `Docker` (as a generic concept), `docker` (as the command-line executable). If a proper name is used which has mixed capitalization like `eBPF` that should be preserved in a longer constant such as `eBPFDelegation`.
Constraint(s): When an acronym or initialism each letter in the acronym should be uppercase, such as with `ClientIP` or `TCPDelay`.
Interpretation: 頭字語の各文字は大文字にすべきである（例: ClientIP, TCPDelay）．

Original: Units must either be explicit in the field name (e.g., `timeoutSeconds`), or must be specified as part of the value (e.g., `resource.Quantity`). Which approach is preferred is TBD, though currently we use the `fooSeconds` convention for durations.
Constraint(s): Units must either be explicit in the field name (e.g., `timeoutSeconds`), or must be specified as part of the value (e.g., `resource.Quantity`).
Interpretation: 単位はフィールド名に明示するか（例: timeoutSeconds），値の一部として指定しなければならない（例: resource.Quantity）．

Original: All string fields should be checked for maximum length.  If the field has a minimum length, that should also be checked.
Constraint(s): All string fields should be checked for maximum length.
Interpretation: すべての文字列フィールドは最大長のチェックを行うべきである．

Original: * The name of a namespace must be a [DNS_LABEL](https://git.k8s.io/design-proposals-archive/architecture/identifiers.md).
Constraint(s): The name of a namespace must be a DNS_LABEL.
Interpretation: namespace名はDNS_LABELでなければならない．

Original: * PATCH /<resourceNamePlural>/<name> - Selectively modify the specified fields of the resource. See more information [below](#patch-operations).
Constraint(s): API resources should use the traditional REST pattern: PATCH /<resourceNamePlural>/<name> - Selectively modify the specified fields of the resource. See more information below.
Interpretation: PATCH /<リソース名複数形>/<名前>でリソースの指定フィールドを選択的に変更するエンドポイントを提供すべきである．

Original: The standard REST verbs (defined below) MUST return singular JSON objects. Some API endpoints may deviate from the strict REST pattern and return resources that are not singular JSON objects, such as streams of JSON objects or unstructured text log data.
Constraint(s): Some API endpoints may deviate from the strict REST pattern and return resources that are not singular JSON objects, such as streams of JSON objects or unstructured text log data.
Interpretation: 一部のAPIエンドポイントは厳密なRESTパターンから逸脱し，JSONストリームや構造化されていないログデータを返してもよい．

Original: Sometimes, when a new type is created, the api designer may anticipate that a union will be needed in the future, even if only one field is allowed initially. In this case, be sure to make the field [Optional](#optional-vs-required) In the validation, you may still return an error if the sole field is unset. Do not set a default value for that field.
Constraint(s): In this case, be sure to make the field Optional In the validation, you may still return an error if the sole field is unset.
Interpretation: 将来のunion化を見越す場合，フィールドをOptionalにしておく．バリデーションでは唯一のフィールドが未設定の場合にエラーを返してもよい．

Original: All API within Kubernetes must leverage constants in this style, including flags and configuration files. Where inconsistent constants were previously used, new flags should be CamelCase only, and over time old flags should be updated to accept a CamelCase value alongside the inconsistent constant. Example: the Kubelet accepts a `--topology-manager-policy` flag that has values `none`, `best-effort`, `restricted`, and `single-numa-node`. This flag should accept `None`, `BestEffort`, `Restricted`, and `SingleNUMANode` going forward. If new values are added to the flag, both forms should be supported.
Constraint(s): Where inconsistent constants were previously used, new flags should be CamelCase only, and over time old flags should be updated to accept a CamelCase value alongside the inconsistent constant.
Interpretation: 新しいフラグはCamelCaseのみとし，既存の不整合なフラグも時間をかけてCamelCase値を受け入れるよう更新すべきである．

Original: The PUT and POST verbs on objects MUST ignore the `status` values, to avoid accidentally overwriting the `status` in read-modify-write scenarios. A `/status` subresource MUST be provided to enable system components to update statuses of resources they manage.
Constraint(s): The PUT and POST verbs on objects MUST ignore the `status` values, to avoid accidentally overwriting the `status` in read-modify-write scenarios.
Interpretation: PUT/POSTはstatusの値を無視しなければならない．read-modify-writeシナリオでstatusが意図せず上書きされるのを防ぐため．

Original: * Go field names must be PascalCase. JSON field names must be camelCase. Other than capitalization of the initial letter, the two should almost always match. No underscores or dashes in either.
Constraint(s): Go field names must be PascalCase. JSON field names must be camelCase. Other than capitalization of the initial letter, the two should almost always match. No underscores or dashes in either.
Interpretation: Goフィールド名はPascalCase，JSONフィールド名はcamelCaseでなければならない．先頭大文字以外は一致すべきで，アンダースコアやダッシュは不可．

Original: All API within Kubernetes must leverage constants in this style, including flags and configuration files. Where inconsistent constants were previously used, new flags should be CamelCase only, and over time old flags should be updated to accept a CamelCase value alongside the inconsistent constant. Example: the Kubelet accepts a `--topology-manager-policy` flag that has values `none`, `best-effort`, `restricted`, and `single-numa-node`. This flag should accept `None`, `BestEffort`, `Restricted`, and `SingleNUMANode` going forward. If new values are added to the flag, both forms should be supported.
Constraint(s): All API within Kubernetes must leverage constants in this style, including flags and configuration files.
Interpretation: Kubernetes内のすべてのAPIは，フラグや設定ファイルを含め，CamelCaseスタイルの定数を使用しなければならない．

Original: - Key names should be all lowercase, with words separated by dashes instead of camelCase
Constraint(s): Key names should be all lowercase, with words separated by dashes instead of camelCase.
Interpretation: ラベル/annotationsのキー名はすべて小文字で，camelCaseではなくダッシュで単語を区切るべきである．

Original: Controllers should assume that a version of a resource may change, and include appropriate error handling.
Constraint(s): Controllers should assume that a version of a resource may change, and include appropriate error handling.
Interpretation: コントローラーはリソースのバージョンが変わりうることを前提とし，適切なエラーハンドリングを含めるべきである．

Original: In addition, all lists that return objects with labels should support label filtering (see [the labels documentation](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels/)), and most lists should support filtering by fields (see [the fields documentation](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/field-selectors/)).
Constraint(s): In addition, all lists that return objects with labels should support label filtering (see the labels documentation), and most lists should support filtering by fields (see the fields documentation).
Interpretation: ラベル付きオブジェクトを返すリストはラベルフィルタリングをサポートすべきであり，ほとんどのリストはフィールドフィルタリングもサポートすべきである．

Original: When the field type has a built-in `nil` value, such as a map or a slice, and your use case means that you need to be able to distinguish between "field not set" and "field set to an empty value", you should use a pointer to the type, even though it has a built-in `nil` value. See https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/34641.
Constraint(s): When the field type has a built-in `nil` value, such as a map or a slice, and your use case means that you need to be able to distinguish between "field not set" and "field set to an empty value", you should use a pointer to the type, even though it has a built-in `nil` value.
Interpretation: map/sliceなど組み込みnil値を持つ型でも，「未設定」と「空値」を区別する必要がある場合はポインタ型を使用すべきである．

Original: An API may represent a single entity in different ways for different clients, or transform an object after certain transitions in the system occur. In these cases, one request object may have two representations available as different resources, or different kinds.
Constraint(s): In these cases, one request object may have two representations available as different resources, or different kinds.
Interpretation: 1つのリクエストオブジェクトが異なるリソースまたは異なるkindとして2つの表現を持つことがある．

Original: * The name of a field referring to another resource of kind `Foo` by name should be called `fooName`. The name of a field referring to another resource of kind `Foo` by ObjectReference (or subset thereof) should be called `fooRef`.
Constraint(s): The name of a field referring to another resource of kind `Foo` by name should be called `fooName`. The name of a field referring to another resource of kind `Foo` by ObjectReference (or subset thereof) should be called `fooRef`.
Interpretation: 別リソースFooを名前で参照するフィールドはfooName，ObjectReferenceで参照する場合はfooRefと命名すべきである．

Original: * GET /<resourceNamePlural> - Retrieve a list of type <resourceName>, e.g. GET /pods returns a list of Pods.
Constraint(s): API resources should use the traditional REST pattern: GET /<resourceNamePlural> - Retrieve a list of type <resourceName>, e.g. GET /pods returns a list of Pods.
Interpretation: APIリソースはREST慣例に従い，GET /<リソース名複数形>でリスト取得エンドポイントを提供すべきである．

Original: The only way for a client to know the expected value of resourceVersion is to have received it from the server in response to a prior operation, typically a GET. This value MUST be treated as opaque by clients and passed unmodified back to the server. Clients should not assume that the resource version has meaning across namespaces, different kinds of resources, or different servers. Currently, the value of resourceVersion is set to match etcd's sequencer. You could think of it as a logical clock the API server can use to order requests. However, we expect the implementation of resourceVersion to change in the future, such as in the case we shard the state by kind and/or namespace, or port to another storage system.
Constraint(s): This value MUST be treated as opaque by clients and passed unmodified back to the server.
Interpretation: resourceVersionの値はクライアントが不透明として扱い，変更せずにサーバーに返さなければならない．

Original: * Controllers should apply their conditions to a resource the first time they visit the resource, even if the `status` is Unknown. This allows other components in the system to know that the condition exists and the controller is making progress on reconciling that resource.
Constraint(s): Controllers should apply their conditions to a resource the first time they visit the resource, even if the `status` is Unknown. This allows other components in the system to know that the condition exists and the controller is making progress on reconciling that resource.
Interpretation: コントローラーは初回のリソース訪問時にconditionを設定すべきである（statusがUnknownでも）．他のコンポーネントがreconciliation進行中であることを把握できる．

Original: The standard REST verbs (defined below) MUST return singular JSON objects. Some API endpoints may deviate from the strict REST pattern and return resources that are not singular JSON objects, such as streams of JSON objects or unstructured text log data.
Constraint(s): The standard REST verbs (defined below) MUST return singular JSON objects.
Interpretation: 標準RESTメソッド（GET/POST/PUT/DELETE/PATCH）は単一のJSONオブジェクトを返さなければならない．

Original: The only way for a client to know the expected value of resourceVersion is to have received it from the server in response to a prior operation, typically a GET. This value MUST be treated as opaque by clients and passed unmodified back to the server. Clients should not assume that the resource version has meaning across namespaces, different kinds of resources, or different servers. Currently, the value of resourceVersion is set to match etcd's sequencer. You could think of it as a logical clock the API server can use to order requests. However, we expect the implementation of resourceVersion to change in the future, such as in the case we shard the state by kind and/or namespace, or port to another storage system.
Constraint(s): Clients should not assume that the resource version has meaning across namespaces, different kinds of resources, or different servers.
Interpretation: クライアントはresourceVersionがnamespace間，異なるリソース間，異なるサーバー間で意味を持つと仮定すべきではない．

Original: Most objects defined in the system should have an endpoint that returns the full set of resources, as well as zero or more endpoints that return subsets of the full list. Some objects may be singletons (the current user, the system defaults) and may not have lists.
Constraint(s): Most objects defined in the system should have an endpoint that returns the full set of resources, as well as zero or more endpoints that return subsets of the full list.
Interpretation: システム内のオブジェクトは，全リソースを返すエンドポイントと，サブセットを返すエンドポイントを持つべきである．

Original: This schema should only be used when the intention is to always have the reference only be to a single resource. If extending to multiple resource types is possible, use the [multiple resource reference](#multiple-resource-reference).
Constraint(s): This schema should only be used when the intention is to always have the reference only be to a single resource.
Interpretation: 単一リソース参照スキーマは，参照先が常に単一リソースであることが意図される場合にのみ使用すべきである．

Original: Otherwise, PUT expects the whole object to be specified. Therefore, if a field is omitted it is assumed that the client wants to clear that field's value. The PUT verb does not accept partial updates. Modification of just part of an object may be achieved by GETting the resource, modifying part of the spec, labels, or annotations, and then PUTting it back. See [concurrency control](#concurrency-control-and-consistency), below, regarding read-modify-write consistency when using this pattern. Some objects may expose alternative resource representations that allow mutation of the status, or performing custom actions on the object.
Constraint(s): Modification of just part of an object may be achieved by GETting the resource, modifying part of the spec, labels, or annotations, and then PUTting it back.
Interpretation: オブジェクトの一部の変更は，GET→spec/labels/annotationsの修正→PUTの手順で行ってもよい．

Original: All list fields should have their "listType" tag(s) set.  Lists with set or map semantics should be checked for uniqueness (declarative validation will do this authomatically).
Constraint(s): Lists with set or map semantics should be checked for uniqueness (declarative validation will do this authomatically).
Interpretation: setまたはmapセマンティクスを持つリストは一意性をチェックすべきである（宣言的バリデーションでは自動で行われる）．

Original: * All numbers (e.g. `int32`, `int64`) are converted to `float64` by Javascript and some other languages, so any field which is expected to exceed that either in magnitude or in precision (e.g. integer values > 53 bits) should be serialized and accepted as strings. `int64` fields must be bounds-checked to be within the range of `-(2^53) < x < (2^53)`.
Constraint(s): All numbers (e.g. `int32`, `int64`) are converted to `float64` by Javascript and some other languages, so any field which is expected to exceed that either in magnitude or in precision (e.g. integer values > 53 bits) should be serialized and accepted as strings. `int64` fields must be bounds-checked to be within the range of `-(2^53) < x < (2^53)`.
Interpretation: JavaScriptなどではすべての数値がfloat64に変換されるため，53ビット超の整数フィールドは文字列としてシリアライズすべきであり，int64は-(2^53) < x < (2^53)の範囲チェックが必要．

Original: Many API types include values that are allocated on behalf of the user from some larger space (e.g. IP addresses from a range, or storage bucket names). These allocations are usually driven by controllers asynchronously to the user's API operations.  Sometimes the user can request a specific value and a controller must confirm or reject that request.  There are many examples of this in Kubernetes, and there a handful of patterns used to represent it.
Constraint(s): Sometimes the user can request a specific value and a controller must confirm or reject that request.
Interpretation: ユーザーが特定の値を要求した場合，コントローラーはその要求を確認または拒否しなければならない．

Original: - In these cases, not using `omitempty` provides the same result, but pollutes the marshaled object with zero values and is not recommended.
Constraint(s): In these cases, not using `omitempty` provides the same result, but pollutes the marshaled object with zero values and is not recommended.
Interpretation: omitemptyを使用しないと，マーシャルされたオブジェクトがゼロ値で汚染されるため推奨されない．

Original: * When asserting a formatting requirement in the negative, use "must not". Example: "must not contain '..'".  Words like "should not" imply that the assertion is optional, and must be avoided.
Constraint(s): When asserting a formatting requirement in the negative, use "must not". Example: "must not contain '..'". Words like "should not" imply that the assertion is optional, and must be avoided.
Interpretation: エラーメッセージで否定的なフォーマット要件を示す場合は「must not」を使用する（例: must not contain '..'）．

Original: Some fields will have a list of allowed values (enumerations). These values will be strings, and they will be in CamelCase, with an initial uppercase letter. Examples: `ClusterFirst`, `Pending`, `ClientIP`. When an acronym or initialism each letter in the acronym should be uppercase, such as with `ClientIP` or `TCPDelay`. When a proper name or the name of a command-line executable is used as a constant the proper name should be represented in consistent casing - examples: `systemd`, `iptables`, `IPVS`, `cgroupfs`, `Docker` (as a generic concept), `docker` (as the command-line executable). If a proper name is used which has mixed capitalization like `eBPF` that should be preserved in a longer constant such as `eBPFDelegation`.
Constraint(s): If a proper name is used which has mixed capitalization like `eBPF` that should be preserved in a longer constant such as `eBPFDelegation`.
Interpretation: eBPFのように大文字小文字が混在する固有名詞は，定数名中でもそのケーシングを維持すべきである（例: eBPFDelegation）．

Original: In some cases, these values may be conditional on or deterministically derived from other fields (e.g. "if otherField is X then this field defaults to 0" or "this field defaults to the value of otherField").  Note that such derived defaults present a hazard in the face of updates - if the "other" field changes, the derived field may have to change, too.  The static defaulting logic is unaware of updates and has no concept of "previous value", which means this inter-field relationship becomes the user's problem - they must update both the field they care about and the "other" field.
Constraint(s): In some cases, these values may be conditional on or deterministically derived from other fields (e.g. "if otherField is X then this field defaults to 0" or "this field defaults to the value of otherField").
Interpretation: デフォルト値は他のフィールドの値に条件付きまたは決定論的に依存してもよい（例: otherFieldがXならデフォルト0）．

Original: * kind: a string that identifies the schema this object should have
Constraint(s): All JSON objects returned by an API MUST include a `kind` field that identifies the schema this object should have.
Interpretation: APIが返すすべてのJSONオブジェクトには，そのスキーマを識別するkindフィールドを含めなければならない．

Original: - Required structs should use `omitzero` to avoid marshalling the zero value.
Constraint(s): Required structs should use `omitzero` to avoid marshalling the zero value.
Interpretation: requiredな構造体フィールドはomitzeroを使用してゼロ値のマーシャリングを避けるべきである．

Original: The status object is encoded as JSON and provided as the body of the response. The status object contains fields for humans and machine consumers of the API to get more detailed information for the cause of the failure. The information in the status object supplements, but does not override, the HTTP status code's meaning. When fields in the status object have the same meaning as generally defined HTTP headers and that header is returned with the response, the header should be considered as having higher priority.
Constraint(s): When fields in the status object have the same meaning as generally defined HTTP headers and that header is returned with the response, the header should be considered as having higher priority.
Interpretation: statusオブジェクトのフィールドがHTTPヘッダーと同じ意味を持つ場合，ヘッダーの方が優先されるべきである．

Original: The fieldPath should point to a single value, and use [the recommended field selector notation](#selecting-fields) to denote the field path.
Constraint(s): The fieldPath should point to a single value, and use the recommended field selector notation to denote the field path.
Interpretation: fieldPathは単一の値を指し，推奨されるフィールドセレクタ記法を使用すべきである．

Original: Therefore, resources supporting auto-generation of unique labels should have a `uniqueLabelKey` field, so that the user could specify the key if they wanted to, but if unspecified, it could be set by default, such as to the resource type, like job, deployment, or replicationController. The value would need to be at least spatially unique, and perhaps temporally unique in the case of job.
Constraint(s): Therefore, resources supporting auto-generation of unique labels should have a `uniqueLabelKey` field, so that the user could specify the key if they wanted to, but if unspecified, it could be set by default, such as to the resource type, like job, deployment, or replicationController.
Interpretation: ユニークラベルの自動生成をサポートするリソースは，ユーザーがキーを指定できるuniqueLabelKeyフィールドを持つべきである．

Original: Conditions should follow the standard schema included in [k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1/types.go](https://github.com/kubernetes/apimachinery/blob/release-1.23/pkg/apis/meta/v1/types.go#L1432-L1492). It should be included as a top level element in status, similar to
Constraint(s): Conditions should be included as a top level element in `status`.
Interpretation: conditionsはstatusのトップレベル要素として含めるべきである（ネストされた場所ではなく）．

Original: * GET /<resourceNamePlural>/<name> - Retrieves a single resource with the given name, e.g. GET /pods/first returns a Pod named 'first'. Should be constant time, and the resource should be bounded in size.
Constraint(s): API resources should use the traditional REST pattern: GET /<resourceNamePlural>/<name> - Retrieves a single resource with the given name, e.g. GET /pods/first returns a Pod named 'first'. Should be constant time, and the resource should be bounded in size.
Interpretation: GET /<リソース名複数形>/<名前>で単一リソースを取得するエンドポイントを提供すべきである．定数時間で完了し，レスポンスサイズは有界であるべき．

Original: * When asserting a requirement in the positive, use "must".  Examples: "must be greater than 0", "must match regex '[a-z]+'".  Words like "should" imply that the assertion is optional, and must be avoided.
Constraint(s): When asserting a requirement in the positive, use "must". Examples: "must be greater than 0", "must match regex '[a-z]+'". Words like "should" imply that the assertion is optional, and must be avoided.
Interpretation: エラーメッセージで肯定的な要件を示す場合は「must」を使用する（例: must be greater than 0）．「should」は任意を暗示するため避ける．

Original: All map fields should be checked for maximum size.  If the map has a minimum size, that should also be checked.
Constraint(s): If the map has a minimum size, that should also be checked.
Interpretation: mapに最小サイズがある場合，その最小サイズもチェックすべきである．

Original: - Such keys are effectively part of the kubernetes API and may be subject to deprecation and compatibility policies.
Constraint(s): Such keys are effectively part of the kubernetes API and may be subject to deprecation and compatibility policies.
Interpretation: そのようなキーは実質的にKubernetes APIの一部であり，非推奨化と互換性ポリシーの対象となりうる．

Original: Labels are the domain of users. They are intended to facilitate organization and management of API resources using attributes that are meaningful to users, as opposed to meaningful to the system. Think of them as user-created mp3 or email inbox labels, as opposed to the directory structure used by a program to store its data. The former enables the user to apply an arbitrary ontology, whereas the latter is implementation-centric and inflexible. Users will use labels to select resources to operate on, display label values in CLI/UI columns, etc. Users should always retain full power and flexibility over the label schemas they apply to labels in their namespaces.
Constraint(s): Users will use labels to select resources to operate on, display label values in CLI/UI columns, etc. Users should always retain full power and flexibility over the label schemas they apply to labels in their namespaces.
Interpretation: ユーザーは自分のnamespace内のラベルスキーマに対して完全な権限と柔軟性を保持すべきである．

Original: - Adding keys to maps
Constraint(s): All forms of defaulting should only add keys to maps.
Interpretation: すべてのデフォルト設定は，mapにキーを追加するのみとすべきである（既存キーの上書き禁止）．

Original: As the permissions of the controller can differ from the permissions of the author of the object the controller is managing, it is possible that the author of the object may not have permissions to view the referred object. As a result, the copying of any values about the referred object to the referrer object can be considered permissions escalations, enabling a user to read values that they would not have access to previously.
Constraint(s): As the permissions of the controller can differ from the permissions of the author of the object the controller is managing, it is possible that the author of the object may not have permissions to view the referred object.
Interpretation: コントローラーの権限とオブジェクト作成者の権限が異なるため，作成者が参照先オブジェクトの閲覧権限を持たない可能性がある．

Original: - Key prefixes should carry as much context as possible.
Constraint(s): Key prefixes should carry as much context as possible.
Interpretation: キープレフィックスはできるだけ多くのコンテキストを伝えるべきである．

Original: All objects that represent a physical resource whose state may vary from the user's desired intent SHOULD have a `spec` and a `status`. Objects whose state cannot vary from the user's desired intent MAY have only `spec`, and MAY rename `spec` to a more appropriate name.
Constraint(s): Objects whose state cannot vary from the user's desired intent MAY have only `spec`, and MAY rename `spec` to a more appropriate name.
Interpretation: ユーザーの意図と状態が常に一致するオブジェクトはspecのみでもよく，specをより適切な名前にリネームしてもよい．

Original: * PUT /<resourceNamePlural>/<name> - Update or create the resource with the given name with the JSON object provided by the client. Whether a resource can be created with a PUT request depends on the particular resource's storage strategy configuration, specifically the `AllowCreateOnUpdate()` return value. Most built-in types do not allow this.
Constraint(s): API resources should use the traditional REST pattern: PUT /<resourceNamePlural>/<name> - Update or create the resource with the given name with the JSON object provided by the client. Whether a resource can be created with a PUT request depends on the particular resource's storage strategy configuration, specifically the `AllowCreateOnUpdate()` return value. Most built-in types do not allow this.
Interpretation: PUT /<リソース名複数形>/<名前>でリソースの更新（または作成）エンドポイントを提供すべきである．PUTでの作成はAllowCreateOnUpdate()の設定による．

Original: API objects are validated upon receipt by the apiserver. Validation can be implemented in two ways: declaratively, using tags on the Go type definitions, or manually, by writing validation functions. For all new APIs, declarative validation is the preferred approach for the validation rules it supports.  For more information see the [declarative validation documentation](api_changes.md#declarative-validation). Validation errors are flagged and returned to the caller in a `Failure` status, usually with `reason` set to `Invalid`.
Constraint(s): For all new APIs, declarative validation is the preferred approach for the validation rules it supports.
Interpretation: すべての新しいAPIでは，宣言的バリデーションが推奨されるアプローチである．

Original: An API may represent a single entity in different ways for different clients, or transform an object after certain transitions in the system occur. In these cases, one request object may have two representations available as different resources, or different kinds.
Constraint(s): An API may represent a single entity in different ways for different clients, or transform an object after certain transitions in the system occur.
Interpretation: APIは同一エンティティを異なるクライアント向けに異なる方法で表現してもよい．

Original: When the user chooses to change the `discriminator` value (e.g., from `Type X` to `Type Y`) without changing any other fields then the system should clear the fields that were used to represent `Type X` in the union along with releasing resources that were attached to `Type X`. This should automatically happen irrespective of how these values and resources were allocated (i.e., reserved by the user or automatically allocated by the system. A concrete example of this is again `Service` API. The system allocates resources such as `NodePorts` and `ClusterIPs` and automatically fill in the fields that represent them in case of the service is of type `NodePort` or `ClusterIP` (`discriminator` values). These resources and the fields representing them are automatically cleared when  the users changes service type to `ExternalName` where these resources and field values no longer apply.
Constraint(s): When the user chooses to change the `discriminator` value (e.g., from `Type X` to `Type Y`) without changing any other fields then the system should clear the fields that were used to represent `Type X` in the union along with releasing resources that were attached to `Type X`.
Interpretation: discriminator値の変更時，システムは以前の型のフィールドをクリアし，関連リソースを解放すべきである．

Original: * uid: a unique in time and space value (typically an RFC 4122 generated identifier, see [the identifiers docs](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/)) used to distinguish between objects with the same name that have been deleted and recreated
Constraint(s): Every object kind MUST have a `metadata.uid` field that uniquely distinguishes objects with the same name that have been deleted and recreated.
Interpretation: すべてのオブジェクトkindは，削除・再作成されたオブジェクトを区別するためのmetadata.uidフィールドを持たなければならない．

Original: The goal is that, for a given input (e.g. YAML file), PUT on an existing object should produce the same result as if you used that input to create the object. Calling PUT a second time with the same input should be idempotent and should not change the resource.  Even a read-modify-write cycle is not a perfect solution in the face of version skew.
Constraint(s): Calling PUT a second time with the same input should be idempotent and should not change the resource.
Interpretation: 同じ入力でPUTを2回呼び出した場合，冪等であり，リソースを変更すべきではない．

Original: Like admission controlled defaults, these controllers may consider external state when deciding what values to set, must handle race conditions, and can be disabled.  Fields which are initialized this way must be strictly optional (meaning observers will see the object without these fields set, and that is allowable and semantically correct).
Constraint(s): Fields which are initialized this way must be strictly optional (meaning observers will see the object without these fields set, and that is allowable and semantically correct).
Interpretation: 遅延初期化で設定されるフィールドは厳密にoptionalでなければならない（フィールド未設定の状態が観測されうる）．

Original: * DELETE /<resourceNamePlural>/<name>  - Delete the single resource with the given name. DeleteOptions may specify gracePeriodSeconds, the optional duration in seconds before the object should be deleted. Individual kinds may declare fields which provide a default grace period, and different kinds may have differing kind-wide default grace periods. A user provided grace period overrides a default grace period, including the zero grace period ("now").
Constraint(s): API resources should use the traditional REST pattern: DELETE /<resourceNamePlural>/<name> - Delete the single resource with the given name. DeleteOptions may specify gracePeriodSeconds, the optional duration in seconds before the object should be deleted. Individual kinds may declare fields which provide a default grace period, and different kinds may have differing kind-wide default grace periods. A user provided grace period overrides a default grace period, including the zero grace period ("now").
Interpretation: DELETE /<リソース名複数形>/<名前>で単一リソースを削除するエンドポイントを提供すべきである．DeleteOptionsでgracePeriodSecondsを指定可能．

Original: Field references differ from other reference types, as the operator has no knowledge of the object prior to the reference. Since the schema of an object can differ for different versions of a resource, this means that a “version” is required for this type of reference.
Constraint(s): Since the schema of an object can differ for different versions of a resource, this means that a “version” is required for this type of reference.
Interpretation: オブジェクトのスキーマはバージョンにより異なるため，フィールド参照にはバージョンの指定が必要である．

Original: All objects that represent a physical resource whose state may vary from the user's desired intent SHOULD have a `spec` and a `status`. Objects whose state cannot vary from the user's desired intent MAY have only `spec`, and MAY rename `spec` to a more appropriate name.
Constraint(s): All objects that represent a physical resource whose state may vary from the user's desired intent SHOULD have a `spec` and a `status`.
Interpretation: ユーザーの意図と実際の状態が異なりうる物理リソースを表すオブジェクトは，specとstatusの両方を持つべきである．

Original: In some cases, these values may be conditional on or deterministically derived from other fields (e.g. "if otherField is X then this field defaults to 0" or "this field defaults to the value of otherField").  Note that such derived defaults present a hazard in the face of updates - if the "other" field changes, the derived field may have to change, too.  The static defaulting logic is unaware of updates and has no concept of "previous value", which means this inter-field relationship becomes the user's problem - they must update both the field they care about and the "other" field.
Constraint(s): The static defaulting logic is unaware of updates and has no concept of "previous value", which means this inter-field relationship becomes the user's problem - they must update both the field they care about and the "other" field.
Interpretation: 静的デフォルトのロジックは更新を認識せず「以前の値」の概念がないため，フィールド間の依存関係はユーザーが管理しなければならない．

Original: A field that is required means that the writer must express an opinion about the value of the field. Required fields are always present, and readers can rely on the field being present in the object.
Constraint(s): A field that is required means that the writer must express an opinion about the value of the field.
Interpretation: requiredフィールドでは，書き手がそのフィールドの値について意見を表明しなければならない（必ず値を指定する）．

Original: * All public integer fields MUST use the Go `int32` or Go `int64` types, not `int` (which is ambiguously sized, depending on target platform).  Internal types may use `int`.
Constraint(s): All public integer fields MUST use the Go `int32` or Go `int64` types, not `int` (which is ambiguously sized, depending on target platform). Internal types may use `int`.
Interpretation: 公開整数フィールドは必ずGoのint32またはint64を使用しなければならない．int型はプラットフォーム依存でサイズが曖昧なため禁止．内部型ではintを使用可．

Original: In this scenario, the user will supply all of the required path elements: group, version, resource, name, and possibly namespace. As such, the controller can construct the API prefix and query it without the use of the discovery client:
Constraint(s): In this scenario, the user will supply all of the required path elements: group, version, resource, name, and possibly namespace.
Interpretation: ユーザーはgroup，version，resource，name，および必要に応じてnamespaceのすべてのパス要素を指定する．

Original: * When designing Conditions for a resource, it's helpful to have a common top-level condition which summarizes more detailed conditions. Simple consumers may simply query the top-level condition. Although they are not a consistent standard, the `Ready` and `Succeeded` condition types may be used by API designers for long-running and bounded-execution objects, respectively.
Constraint(s): When designing Conditions for a resource, it's helpful to have a common top-level condition which summarizes more detailed conditions. Simple consumers may simply query the top-level condition. Although they are not a consistent standard, the `Ready` and `Succeeded` condition types may be used by API designers for long-running and bounded-execution objects, respectively.
Interpretation: リソースのCondition設計時，詳細なconditionを要約する共通のトップレベルconditionがあると便利．長期実行にはReady，有限実行にはSucceededを使用できる．

Original: Condition `status` values may be `True`, `False`, or `Unknown`. The absence of a condition should be interpreted the same as `Unknown`.  How controllers handle `Unknown` depends on the Condition in question.
Constraint(s): Condition `status` values may be `True`, `False`, or `Unknown`.
Interpretation: conditionのstatus値はTrue，False，Unknownのいずれかである．

Original: * When referencing a literal string value, indicate the literal in single-quotes. Example: "must not contain '..'".
Constraint(s): When referencing a literal string value, indicate the literal in single-quotes. Example: "must not contain '..'".
Interpretation: エラーメッセージでリテラル文字列を参照する場合はシングルクォートで囲む（例: must not contain '..'）．

Original: Events are complementary to status information, since they can provide some historical information about status and occurrences in addition to current or previous status. Generate events for situations users or administrators should be alerted about.
Constraint(s): Generate events for situations users or administrators should be alerted about.
Interpretation: ユーザーや管理者に警告すべき状況についてイベントを生成すべきである．

Original: The operator would be expected to find the resource via the discovery client (as the version is not supplied). As any retrievable field would be common to all objects, any version of the resource should do.
Constraint(s): As any retrievable field would be common to all objects, any version of the resource should do.
Interpretation: 取得可能なフィールドはすべてのオブジェクトに共通のため，リソースのどのバージョンでも十分である．

Original: - Unprefixed keys are reserved for end-users.  All other labels and annotations must be prefixed.
Constraint(s): Unprefixed keys are reserved for end-users. All other labels and annotations must be prefixed.
Interpretation: プレフィックスなしのキーはエンドユーザー用に予約されている．それ以外のすべてのラベルとannotationsにはプレフィックスが必要．

Original: Future versions of Kubernetes may allow alternative encodings of objects beyond JSON.
Constraint(s): Future versions of Kubernetes may allow alternative encodings of objects beyond JSON.
Interpretation: Kubernetesの将来のバージョンでは，JSON以外のオブジェクトエンコーディングが許可される可能性がある．

Original: Objects may report multiple conditions, and new types of conditions may be added in the future or by 3rd party controllers. Therefore, conditions are represented using a list/slice of objects, where each condition has a similar structure. This collection should be treated as a map with a key of `type`.
Constraint(s): This collection should be treated as a map with a key of `type`.
Interpretation: conditionsのコレクションはtypeをキーとするマップとして扱うべきである．

Original: Problems with uids and hashes, however, include that they have no semantic meaning to the user, are not memorable nor readily recognizable, and are not predictable. Lack of predictability obstructs use cases such as creation of a replication controller from a pod, such as people want to do when exploring the system, bootstrapping a self-hosted cluster, or deletion and re-creation of a new RC that adopts the pods of the previous one, such as to rename it. Generation numbers are more predictable and much clearer, assuming there is a logical sequence. Fortunately, for deployments that's the case. For jobs, use of creation timestamps is common internally. Users should always be able to turn off auto-generation, in order to permit some of the scenarios described above. Note that auto-generated labels will also become one more field that needs to be stripped out when cloning a resource, within a namespace, in a new namespace, in a new cluster, etc., and will need to be ignored around when updating a resource via patch or read-modify-write sequence.
Constraint(s): Users should always be able to turn off auto-generation, in order to permit some of the scenarios described above.
Interpretation: ユーザーは常にラベルの自動生成を無効化できるべきである．

Original: - "kubernetes.io" is the preferred form for labels and annotations, "k8s.io" should not be used for new map keys.
Constraint(s): "kubernetes.io" is the preferred form for labels and annotations, "k8s.io" should not be used for new map keys.
Interpretation: ラベルとannotationsにはkubernetes.ioが推奨形式であり，新しいマップキーにk8s.ioを使用すべきではない．

Original: * Conditions should be added to explicitly convey properties that users and components care about rather than requiring those properties to be inferred from other observations.  Once defined, the meaning of a Condition can not be changed arbitrarily - it becomes part of the API, and has the same backwards- and forwards-compatibility concerns of any other part of the API.
Constraint(s): Conditions should be added to explicitly convey properties that users and components care about rather than requiring those properties to be inferred from other observations. Once defined, the meaning of a Condition can not be changed arbitrarily - it becomes part of the API, and has the same backwards- and forwards-compatibility concerns of any other part of the API.
Interpretation: Conditionは推論に頼らず，ユーザーやコンポーネントが気にするプロパティを明示的に伝えるべきである．一度定義するとAPIの一部となり，後方互換性の対象になる．

Original: All list fields should have their "listType" tag(s) set.  Lists with set or map semantics should be checked for uniqueness (declarative validation will do this authomatically).
Constraint(s): All list fields should have their "listType" tag(s) set.
Interpretation: すべてのリストフィールドにlistTypeタグを設定すべきである．

Original: Names generated by the system may be requested using `metadata.generateName`. GenerateName indicates that the name should be made unique by the server prior to persisting it. A non-empty value for the field indicates the server should attempt to make the name unique (and the name returned to the client will be different than the name passed). The value of this field will be combined with a random suffix on the server if the Name field has not been provided. The provided value must be valid within the rules for Name, and may be truncated by the length of the suffix. If this field is specified, and Name is not present, the server will return a 409 with Reason `AlreadyExists` if the generated name exists, and the client should retry (after waiting at least the amount of time indicated in the Retry-After header, if it is present).
Constraint(s): If this field is specified, and Name is not present, the server will return a 409 with Reason `AlreadyExists` if the generated name exists, and the client should retry (after waiting at least the amount of time indicated in the Retry-After header, if it is present).
Interpretation: generateNameが指定されNameが未設定の場合，生成名が既存なら409/AlreadyExistsを返す．クライアントはRetry-Afterヘッダーに従いリトライすべきである．

Original: Map fields should have their keys validated, in addition to their values.
Constraint(s): Map fields should have their keys validated, in addition to their values.
Interpretation: mapフィールドは値だけでなくキーもバリデーションすべきである．

Original: Instead, we can provide a built-in admission controller or a MutatingWebhookConfiguration.  Unlike static defaults, these may consider external state (such as annotations on StorageClass objects) when deciding default values, and must handle things like race conditions (e.g. a StorageClass is designated the default, but the admission controller has not yet seen that update).  These admission controllers are strictly optional and can be disabled.  As such, fields which are initialized this way must be strictly optional.
Constraint(s): Unlike static defaults, these may consider external state (such as annotations on StorageClass objects) when deciding default values, and must handle things like race conditions (e.g. a StorageClass is designated the default, but the admission controller has not yet seen that update).
Interpretation: Admission Controlledデフォルトは外部状態（例: StorageClassのアノテーション）を考慮でき，レースコンディションに対応しなければならない．

Original: * POST /<resourceNamePlural> - Create a new resource from the JSON object provided by the client.
Constraint(s): API resources should use the traditional REST pattern: POST /<resourceNamePlural> - Create a new resource from the JSON object provided by the client.
Interpretation: POST /<リソース名複数形>でクライアント提供のJSONオブジェクトから新リソースを作成するエンドポイントを提供すべきである．

Original: * annotations: a map of string keys and values that can be used by external tooling to store and retrieve arbitrary metadata about this object (see [the annotations docs](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/annotations/))
Constraint(s): Every object SHOULD have a `metadata.annotations` field that external tooling can use to store and retrieve arbitrary metadata about the object.
Interpretation: すべてのオブジェクトは，外部ツールが任意のメタデータを保存・取得するためのmetadata.annotationsフィールドを持つべきである．

Original: Conditions should follow the standard schema included in [k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1/types.go](https://github.com/kubernetes/apimachinery/blob/release-1.23/pkg/apis/meta/v1/types.go#L1432-L1492). It should be included as a top level element in status, similar to
Constraint(s): Conditions should follow the standard schema included in k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1/types.go.
Interpretation: Conditionはk8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1/types.goの標準スキーマに従うべきである．

Original: Every simple kind returned by the server, and any simple kind sent to the server that must support idempotency or optimistic concurrency should return this value. Since simple resources are often used as input alternate actions that modify objects, the resource version of the simple resource should correspond to the resource version of the object.
Constraint(s): Since simple resources are often used as input alternate actions that modify objects, the resource version of the simple resource should correspond to the resource version of the object.
Interpretation: simple resourceのresourceVersionは，操作対象オブジェクトのresourceVersionに対応すべきである．

Original: The PUT and POST verbs on objects MUST ignore the `status` values, to avoid accidentally overwriting the `status` in read-modify-write scenarios. A `/status` subresource MUST be provided to enable system components to update statuses of resources they manage.
Constraint(s): A `/status` subresource MUST be provided to enable system components to update statuses of resources they manage.
Interpretation: システムコンポーネントが管理するリソースのステータスを更新できるよう，/statusサブリソースを必ず提供しなければならない．

Original: Annotations have very different intended usage from labels. They are primarily generated and consumed by tooling and system extensions, or are used by end-users to engage non-standard behavior of components.  For example, an annotation might be used to indicate that an instance of a resource expects additional handling by non-kubernetes controllers. Annotations may carry arbitrary payloads, including JSON documents.  Like labels, annotation keys can be prefixed with a governing domain (e.g. `example.com/key-name`).  Unprefixed keys (e.g. `key-name`) are reserved for end-users.  Third-party components must use prefixed keys.  Key prefixes under the "kubernetes.io" and "k8s.io" domains are reserved for use by the kubernetes project and must not be used by third-parties.
Constraint(s): Third-party components must use prefixed keys.
Interpretation: サードパーティコンポーネントはプレフィックス付きのキーを使用しなければならない．

Original: - Setting previously unset fields
Constraint(s): All forms of defaulting should only set previously unset fields.
Interpretation: すべてのデフォルト設定は，以前に設定されていないフィールドのみを設定すべきである．

Original: Static defaults are the best choice for values which are logically required, but which have a value that works well for most users.  Static defaulting must not consider any state except the object being operated upon (and the complexity of Service API stands as an example of why).
Constraint(s): Static defaults are the best choice for values which are logically required, but which have a value that works well for most users.
Interpretation: 静的デフォルトは，論理的にはrequiredだがほとんどのユーザーに適するデフォルト値がある場合に最適な選択である．

Original: * The name of a field expressing a boolean property called 'fooable' should be called `Fooable`, not `IsFooable`.
Constraint(s): The name of a field expressing a boolean property called 'fooable' should be called `Fooable`, not `IsFooable`.
Interpretation: booleanプロパティfooableを表すフィールド名はFooableであり，IsFooableではない．

Original: All list fields should be checked for maximum size.  If the list has a minimum size, that should also be checked.
Constraint(s): If the list has a minimum size, that should also be checked.
Interpretation: リストに最小サイズがある場合，その最小サイズもチェックすべきである．

Original: * When referencing another field name, indicate the name in back-quotes. Example: "must be greater than \`request\`".
Constraint(s): When referencing another field name, indicate the name in back-quotes. Example: "must be greater than \`request\`".
Interpretation: エラーメッセージで別のフィールド名を参照する場合はバッククォートで囲む（例: must be greater than `request`）．

Original: Some objects which are not persisted in the system - such as `SubjectAccessReview` and other webhook style calls - may choose to add `spec` and `status` to encapsulate a "call and response" pattern. The `spec` is the request (often a request for information) and the `status` is the response. For these RPC like objects the only operation may be POST, but having a consistent schema between submission and response reduces the complexity of these clients.
Constraint(s): For these RPC like objects the only operation may be POST, but having a consistent schema between submission and response reduces the complexity of these clients.
Interpretation: RPCライクなオブジェクトの操作はPOSTのみでもよいが，送信と応答のスキーマを一貫させることでクライアントの複雑さを軽減する．

Original: Kubernetes will always return the `Status` kind from any API endpoint when an error occurs. Clients SHOULD handle these types of objects when appropriate.
Constraint(s): Clients SHOULD handle these types of objects when appropriate.
Interpretation: クライアントは適切な場合にStatus kindオブジェクトを処理すべきである．

Original: Short-name matches may be given lower priority than an exact match of a resource type, so use of short names increases potential for inconsistent behavior in clusters with custom resources installed, if those custom resource types overlap with short names.
Constraint(s): Short-name matches may be given lower priority than an exact match of a resource type, so use of short names increases potential for inconsistent behavior in clusters with custom resources installed, if those custom resource types overlap with short names.
Interpretation: ショートネームのマッチはリソース型の完全一致より優先度が低くなることがあり，カスタムリソースが重複すると動作が不整合になる可能性がある．

Original: Since almost everything is happening asynchronously to almost everything else, controller implementations should take care around the ordering of operations. For example, whether the controller updates a `status` field before or after it actuates a change depends on what guarantees need to be made to observers of the system.  In some cases, writing to a `status` field represents an acknowledgement or acceptance of a `spec` value, and it is OK to write it before actuation.  However, if it would be problematic for a client to observe the `status` value before it is actuated then the controller must actuate first and update `status` afterward.  In some rarer cases, controllers will need to acknowledge, then actuate, then update to a "final" value.
Constraint(s): However, if it would be problematic for a client to observe the `status` value before it is actuated then the controller must actuate first and update `status` afterward.
Interpretation: クライアントがstatus値を実行前に観測すると問題がある場合，コントローラーは先に実行してからstatusを更新しなければならない．

Original: As another example, a "pod status" resource may accept a PUT with the "pod" kind, with different rules about what fields may be changed.
Constraint(s): As another example, a "pod status" resource may accept a PUT with the "pod" kind, with different rules about what fields may be changed.
Interpretation: 例として，pod statusリソースはpod kindでのPUTを受け付け，変更可能なフィールドに異なるルールを適用してもよい．

Original: * Http status code: `500 StatusInternalServerError` `code` may contain the suggested HTTP return code for this status.
Constraint(s): Http status code: `500 StatusInternalServerError` `code` may contain the suggested HTTP return code for this status.
Interpretation: codeフィールドは，このステータスに対する推奨HTTPリターンコードを含んでもよい．

Original: `message` may contain human-readable description of the error
Constraint(s): `message` may contain a human-readable description of the error.
Interpretation: messageフィールドは人間可読なエラー説明を含んでもよい．

Original: Sometimes, when a new type is created, the api designer may anticipate that a union will be needed in the future, even if only one field is allowed initially. In this case, be sure to make the field [Optional](#optional-vs-required) In the validation, you may still return an error if the sole field is unset. Do not set a default value for that field.
Constraint(s): Sometimes, when a new type is created, the api designer may anticipate that a union will be needed in the future, even if only one field is allowed initially.
Interpretation: API設計者は将来のunion化を見越して，初期段階でフィールドが1つしかなくてもunion構造を準備しておくことができる．

Original: In very rare cases, these values may be allocated from some pool or determined by some other method (e.g. Service's IP and IP-family related fields need to consider other configuration settings).
Constraint(s): In very rare cases, these values may be allocated from some pool or determined by some other method (e.g. Service's IP and IP-family related fields need to consider other configuration settings).
Interpretation: まれに，デフォルト値がプールから割り当てられるか他の方法で決定される場合がある（例: ServiceのIPとIPファミリー関連フィールド）．

Original: **Conditions** provide a standard mechanism for higher-level status reporting from a controller. They are an extension mechanism which allows tools and other controllers to collect summary information about resources without needing to understand resource-specific status details. Conditions should complement more detailed information about the observed status of an object written by a controller, rather than replace it. For example, the "Available" condition of a Deployment can be determined by examining `readyReplicas`, `replicas`, and other properties of the Deployment. However, the "Available" condition allows other components to avoid duplicating the availability logic in the Deployment controller.
Constraint(s): Conditions should complement more detailed information about the observed status of an object written by a controller, rather than replace it.
Interpretation: Conditionsは，コントローラーが記録する詳細なステータス情報を補完するものであり，置き換えるものではない．

Original: * The server should set the `Retry-After` HTTP header and return `retryAfterSeconds` in the details field of the object. A value of `0` is the default.
Constraint(s): The server should set the `Retry-After` HTTP header and return `retryAfterSeconds` in the details field of the object. A value of `0` is the default.
Interpretation: サーバーはRetry-After HTTPヘッダーを設定し，detailsフィールドにretryAfterSecondsを返すべきである（別のステータスコードに関する同様の規約）．

Original: All dates should be serialized as RFC3339 strings.
Constraint(s): All dates should be serialized as RFC3339 strings.
Interpretation: すべての日付はRFC 3339形式の文字列としてシリアライズすべきである．

Original: * namespace: a namespace is a DNS compatible label that objects are subdivided into. The default namespace is 'default'. See [the namespace docs](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/namespaces/) for more.
Constraint(s): Every object kind MUST have the following metadata in a nested object field called "metadata": namespace: a namespace is a DNS compatible label that objects are subdivided into. The default namespace is 'default'. See the namespace docs for more.
Interpretation: すべてのオブジェクトkindは，metadata.namespaceフィールドを持たなければならない．デフォルトは'default'．

Original: * Field and resource names should be declarative, not imperative (SomethingDoer, DoneBy, DoneAt).
Constraint(s): Field and resource names should be declarative, not imperative (SomethingDoer, DoneBy, DoneAt).
Interpretation: フィールドとリソースの名前は宣言的であるべきであり，命令的であるべきではない（例: SomethingDoer, DoneBy, DoneAt）．

Original: Condition types should be named in PascalCase. Short condition names are preferred (e.g. "Ready" over "MyResourceReady").
Constraint(s): Condition types should be named in PascalCase.
Interpretation: Condition type名はPascalCaseで命名すべきである（例: Ready, OutOfDisk, Succeeded）．

Original: * resourceVersion: a string that identifies the common version of the objects returned by in a list. This value MUST be treated as opaque by clients and passed unmodified back to the server. A resource version is only valid within a single namespace on a single kind of resource.
Constraint(s): Every list or simple kind SHOULD have the following metadata in a nested object field called "metadata": resourceVersion: a string that identifies the common version of the objects returned by in a list. This value MUST be treated as opaque by clients and passed unmodified back to the server. A resource version is only valid within a single namespace on a single kind of resource.
Interpretation: リスト型またはsimple kindはmetadata.resourceVersionを持つべきであり，この値はクライアントが不透明に扱いサーバーに変更せず返さなければならない（MUST）．

Original: Clients should use the SPDY protocols if their clients have native support, or WebSockets as a fallback. Note that WebSockets is susceptible to Head-of-Line blocking and so clients must read and process each message sequentially. In the future, an HTTP/2 implementation will be exposed that deprecates SPDY.
Constraint(s): Note that WebSockets is susceptible to Head-of-Line blocking and so clients must read and process each message sequentially.
Interpretation: WebSocketsはHead-of-Lineブロッキングの影響を受けるため，クライアントは各メッセージを順次読み取り処理しなければならない．

Original: In general, condition values may change back and forth, but some condition transitions may be monotonic, depending on the resource and condition type. However, conditions are observations and not, themselves, state machines, nor do we define comprehensive state machines for objects, nor behaviors associated with state transitions. The system is level-based rather than edge-triggered, and should assume an Open World.
Constraint(s): The system is level-based rather than edge-triggered, and should assume an Open World.
Interpretation: システムはエッジトリガではなくレベルベースであり，Open Worldを前提とすべきである．

Original: - For `bool` types, the zero value is `false`, which is always a valid user choice. `bool` types should always be pointers, and should always use the `omitempty` tag.
Constraint(s): For `bool` types, the zero value is `false`, which is always a valid user choice. `bool` types should always be pointers, and should always use the `omitempty` tag.
Interpretation: bool型はゼロ値のfalseが常に有効な選択であるため，常にポインタ型（*bool）にし，omitemptyタグを使用すべきである．

Original: New APIs should almost never do this.  Instead, they should use `status`. PersistentVolumes might have been simpler if we had done this.
Constraint(s): Instead, new APIs should use `status`.
Interpretation: 新しいAPIでは割り当て済み値の保持にstatusを使用すべきである．

Original: * deletionTimestamp: a string representing an RFC 3339 date of the date and time after which this resource will be deleted. This field is set by the server when a graceful deletion is requested by the user, and is not directly settable by a client. The resource will be deleted (no longer visible from resource lists, and not reachable by name) after the time in this field except when the object has a finalizer set. In case the finalizer is set the deletion of the object is postponed at least until the finalizer is removed. Once the deletionTimestamp is set, this value may not be unset or be set further into the future, although it may be shortened or the resource may be deleted prior to this time.
Constraint(s): Every object SHOULD have the following metadata in a nested object field called "metadata": deletionTimestamp: a string representing an RFC 3339 date of the date and time after which this resource will be deleted. This field is set by the server when a graceful deletion is requested by the user, and is not directly settable by a client. The resource will be deleted (no longer visible from resource lists, and not reachable by name) after the time in this field except when the object has a finalizer set. In case the finalizer is set the deletion of the object is postponed at least until the finalizer is removed. Once the deletionTimestamp is set, this value may not be unset or be set further into the future, although it may be shortened or the resource may be deleted prior to this time.
Interpretation: すべてのオブジェクトはmetadata.deletionTimestampフィールドを持つべきである．グレースフル削除時にサーバーが設定し，一度設定されると解除や延長はできない．

Original: It is also possible to have the controller choose a version that it finds via the discovery client. However, as schemas can vary across different versions of a resource, the controller must also handle these differences.
Constraint(s): However, as schemas can vary across different versions of a resource, the controller must also handle these differences.
Interpretation: リソースの異なるバージョンでスキーマが異なる場合があるため，コントローラーはこれらの差異に対応しなければならない．

Original: The name of a list kind must end with "List". Lists have a limited set of common metadata. All lists use the required "items" field to contain the array of objects they return. Any kind that has the "items" field must be a list kind.
Constraint(s): The name of a list kind must end with "List".
Interpretation: リスト型のkind名は必ず「List」で終わる必要がある（例: PodList, ServiceList）．

Original: All compatible Kubernetes APIs MUST support "name idempotency" and respond with an HTTP status code 409 when a request is made to POST an object that has the same name as an existing object in the system. See [the identifiers docs](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/) for details.
Constraint(s): All compatible Kubernetes APIs MUST support "name idempotency" and respond with an HTTP status code 409 when a request is made to POST an object that has the same name as an existing object in the system.
Interpretation: 互換性のあるすべてのKubernetes APIは名前の冪等性をサポートし，既存オブジェクトと同名のPOSTリクエストにHTTP 409を返さなければならない．

Original: The "{field}" component should be named to indicate the purpose of the reference. For example, "targetRef" in an endpoint indicates that the object reference specifies the target.
Constraint(s): The "{field}" component should be named to indicate the purpose of the reference.
Interpretation: 参照フィールドの「{field}」部分は参照の目的を示す名前にすべきである．

Original: Annotations have very different intended usage from labels. They are primarily generated and consumed by tooling and system extensions, or are used by end-users to engage non-standard behavior of components.  For example, an annotation might be used to indicate that an instance of a resource expects additional handling by non-kubernetes controllers. Annotations may carry arbitrary payloads, including JSON documents.  Like labels, annotation keys can be prefixed with a governing domain (e.g. `example.com/key-name`).  Unprefixed keys (e.g. `key-name`) are reserved for end-users.  Third-party components must use prefixed keys.  Key prefixes under the "kubernetes.io" and "k8s.io" domains are reserved for use by the kubernetes project and must not be used by third-parties.
Constraint(s): Annotations may carry arbitrary payloads, including JSON documents.
Interpretation: annotationsはJSONドキュメントを含む任意のペイロードを運ぶことができる．

Original: * `fooPeriodSeconds` is preferred for periodic intervals and other waiting periods (e.g., over `fooIntervalSeconds`).
Constraint(s): `fooPeriodSeconds` is preferred for periodic intervals and other waiting periods (e.g., over `fooIntervalSeconds`).
Interpretation: 周期的な間隔にはfooPeriodSecondsが望ましい（fooIntervalSecondsより）．

Original: * Condition type names should make sense for humans; neither positive nor negative polarity can be recommended as a general rule. A negative condition like "MemoryExhausted" may be easier for humans to understand than "SufficientMemory". Conversely, "Ready" or "Succeeded" may be easier to understand than "Failed", because "Failed=Unknown" or "Failed=False" may cause double-negative confusion.
Constraint(s): Condition type names should make sense for humans; neither positive nor negative polarity can be recommended as a general rule. A negative condition like "MemoryExhausted" may be easier for humans to understand than "SufficientMemory". Conversely, "Ready" or "Succeeded" may be easier to understand than "Failed", because "Failed=Unknown" or "Failed=False" may cause double-negative confusion.
Interpretation: Condition type名は人間にとって理解しやすいものであるべきである．正・負のどちらの極性が適切かはケースバイケース（例: MemoryExhausted vs SufficientMemory）．

Original: Annotations have very different intended usage from labels. They are primarily generated and consumed by tooling and system extensions, or are used by end-users to engage non-standard behavior of components.  For example, an annotation might be used to indicate that an instance of a resource expects additional handling by non-kubernetes controllers. Annotations may carry arbitrary payloads, including JSON documents.  Like labels, annotation keys can be prefixed with a governing domain (e.g. `example.com/key-name`).  Unprefixed keys (e.g. `key-name`) are reserved for end-users.  Third-party components must use prefixed keys.  Key prefixes under the "kubernetes.io" and "k8s.io" domains are reserved for use by the kubernetes project and must not be used by third-parties.
Constraint(s): Key prefixes under the "kubernetes.io" and "k8s.io" domains are reserved for use by the kubernetes project and must not be used by third-parties.
Interpretation: kubernetes.ioとk8s.ioドメインのキープレフィックスはKubernetesプロジェクト専用であり，サードパーティは使用してはならない．

Original: 2. A set of N fields, only one of which should be set at any given time - effectively a union.
Constraint(s): A set of N fields, only one of which should be set at any given time - effectively a union.
Interpretation: N個のフィールドのうち任意の時点で1つだけが設定されるべきである（union）．

Original: Status information that may be large (especially proportional in size to collections of other resources, such as lists of references to other objects -- see below) and/or rapidly changing, such as [resource usage](https://git.k8s.io/design-proposals-archive/scheduling/resources.md#usage-data), should be put into separate objects, with possibly a reference from the original object. This helps to ensure that GETs and watch remain reasonably efficient for the majority of clients, which may not need that data.
Constraint(s): Status information that may be large (especially proportional in size to collections of other resources, such as lists of references to other objects -- see below) and/or rapidly changing, such as resource usage, should be put into separate objects, with possibly a reference from the original object.
Interpretation: 大きくなりうるステータス情報（コレクションに比例するデータや急速に変化するデータ）は別オブジェクトに分離し，元のオブジェクトから参照すべきである．

Original: * For state transitions which take a long period of time (e.g. more than 1 minute), it is reasonable to treat the transition itself as an observed state. In these cases, the Condition (such as "Resizing") itself should not be transient, and should instead be signalled using the `True`/`False`/`Unknown` pattern. This allows other observers to determine the last update from the controller, whether successful or failed. In cases where the state transition is unable to complete and continued reconciliation is not feasible, the Reason and Message should be used to indicate that the transition failed.
Constraint(s): For state transitions which take a long period of time (e.g. more than 1 minute), it is reasonable to treat the transition itself as an observed state. In these cases, the Condition (such as "Resizing") itself should not be transient, and should instead be signalled using the `True`/`False`/`Unknown` pattern. This allows other observers to determine the last update from the controller, whether successful or failed. In cases where the state transition is unable to complete and continued reconciliation is not feasible, the Reason and Message should be used to indicate that the transition failed.
Interpretation: 長時間の状態遷移（1分以上）の場合，遷移自体を観測状態として扱いTrue/False/Unknownパターンで表現することが妥当．遷移が完了不能な場合はReasonとMessageで示す．

Original: In general, condition values may change back and forth, but some condition transitions may be monotonic, depending on the resource and condition type. However, conditions are observations and not, themselves, state machines, nor do we define comprehensive state machines for objects, nor behaviors associated with state transitions. The system is level-based rather than edge-triggered, and should assume an Open World.
Constraint(s): In general, condition values may change back and forth, but some condition transitions may be monotonic, depending on the resource and condition type.
Interpretation: conditionの値は一般的に前後に変わりうるが，一部のcondition遷移はリソースやcondition typeに応じて単調になることもある．

Original: * The name of a field that specifies the time at which `something` occurs should be called `somethingTime`. Do not use `stamp` (e.g., `creationTimestamp`).
Constraint(s): The name of a field that specifies the time at which `something` occurs should be called `somethingTime`. Do not use `stamp` (e.g., `creationTimestamp`).
Interpretation: 何かが発生する時刻を指定するフィールドはsomethingTimeと命名すべきである．stamp（例: creationTimestamp）は使用しない．

Original: Never do case-insensitive comparisons in validation.  This is a source of bugs and failed uniqueness assumptions.  If validation treats "abc" and "ABC" as the same, then ALL consumers of that field must do the same, and it is very easy to miss a case.
Constraint(s): If validation treats "abc" and "ABC" as the same, then ALL consumers of that field must do the same, and it is very easy to miss a case.
Interpretation: バリデーションが大文字小文字を同一視する場合，そのフィールドのすべてのコンシューマーも同様にしなければならない．ケースの不一致を見落としやすいため注意が必要．

Original: When adding a field with a static or admission controlled default, this must be considered.  If the field is immutable after creation, consider adding logic to manually "patch" the value from the "old" object into the "new" one when it has been "unset", rather than returning an error or allocating a different value (e.g.  Service IPs).  This will very often be what the user meant, even if it is not what they said.  This may require setting the default in a different way (e.g.  in the registry code which understands updates instead of in the versioned defaulting code which does not).  Be careful to detect and report legitimate errors where the "new" value is specified but is different from the "old" value.
Constraint(s): When adding a field with a static or admission controlled default, this must be considered.
Interpretation: 静的またはAdmission Controlledデフォルトを持つフィールドを追加する際，PUT冪等性への影響を考慮しなければならない．

Original: Types with both `spec` and `status` stanzas can (and usually should) have distinct authorization scopes for them.  This allows users to be granted full write access to `spec` and read-only access to status, while relevant controllers are granted read-only access to `spec` but full write access to status.
Constraint(s): Types with both `spec` and `status` stanzas can (and usually should) have distinct authorization scopes for them.
Interpretation: specとstatusの両方を持つ型は，それぞれに異なる認可スコープを設定できる（通常すべき）．

Original: All string fields should be checked for maximum length.  If the field has a minimum length, that should also be checked.
Constraint(s): If the field has a minimum length, that should also be checked.
Interpretation: フィールドに最小長がある場合，その最小長もチェックすべきである．

Original: * `fooTimeoutSeconds` is preferred for inactivity/unresponsiveness deadlines.
Constraint(s): `fooTimeoutSeconds` is preferred for inactivity/unresponsiveness deadlines.
Interpretation: 非アクティブ/無応答のデッドラインにはfooTimeoutSecondsが望ましい．

Original: Sometimes, at most one of a set of fields can be set.  For example, the [volumes] field of a PodSpec has 17 different volume type-specific fields, such as `nfs` and `iscsi`.  All fields in the set should be [Optional](#optional-vs-required).
Constraint(s): All fields in the set should be Optional.
Interpretation: Union内のすべてのフィールドはOptionalにすべきである．

Original: The Kubernetes API also serves as the foundation for the declarative configuration schema for the system. In order to facilitate level-based operation and expression of declarative configuration, fields in the specification should have declarative rather than imperative names and semantics -- they represent the desired state, not actions intended to yield the desired state.
Constraint(s): In order to facilitate level-based operation and expression of declarative configuration, fields in the specification should have declarative rather than imperative names and semantics -- they represent the desired state, not actions intended to yield the desired state.
Interpretation: specのフィールドは命令的ではなく宣言的な名前とセマンティクスを持つべきである．desired stateを表現し，desired stateに至るアクションを表現しない．

Original: The `status` summarizes the current state of the object in the system, and is usually persisted with the object by automated processes but may be generated on the fly.  As a general guideline, fields in `status` should be the most recent observations of actual state, but they may contain information such as the results of allocations or similar operations which are executed in response to the object's `spec`.  See [below](#representing-allocated-values) for more details.
Constraint(s): As a general guideline, fields in `status` should be the most recent observations of actual state, but they may contain information such as the results of allocations or similar operations which are executed in response to the object's `spec`.
Interpretation: statusのフィールドは実際の状態の最新の観測値であるべきだが，specに応じた操作結果（例: リソース割り当て結果）を含んでもよい．

Original: * generation: a sequence number representing a specific generation of the desired state. Set by the system and monotonically increasing, per-resource. May be compared, such as for RAW and WAW consistency.
Constraint(s): Every object SHOULD have the following metadata in a nested object field called "metadata": generation: a sequence number representing a specific generation of the desired state. Set by the system and monotonically increasing, per-resource. May be compared, such as for RAW and WAW consistency.
Interpretation: すべてのオブジェクトはmetadata.generationフィールドを持つべきである．desired stateの世代を表すシーケンス番号で，リソースごとに単調増加する．

Original: Instead, we can provide a built-in admission controller or a MutatingWebhookConfiguration.  Unlike static defaults, these may consider external state (such as annotations on StorageClass objects) when deciding default values, and must handle things like race conditions (e.g. a StorageClass is designated the default, but the admission controller has not yet seen that update).  These admission controllers are strictly optional and can be disabled.  As such, fields which are initialized this way must be strictly optional.
Constraint(s): As such, fields which are initialized this way must be strictly optional.
Interpretation: Admission Controlledデフォルトで初期化されるフィールドは厳密にoptionalでなければならない．

Original: Categories listed in discovery may be used by clients as hints to resolve user invocations to multiple resources.
Constraint(s): Categories listed in discovery may be used by clients as hints to resolve user invocations to multiple resources.
Interpretation: ディスカバリに記載されたカテゴリは，ユーザー入力を複数のリソースに解決するヒントとして使用可能．

Original: Like all controllers, care must be taken to not clobber unrelated fields or values (e.g. in an array).  Using one of the patch or apply mechanisms is recommended to facilitate composition and concurrency of controllers.
Constraint(s): Like all controllers, care must be taken to not clobber unrelated fields or values (e.g. in an array).
Interpretation: コントローラーは無関係なフィールドや値を上書きしないよう注意しなければならない．

Original: - The API server should allow POSTing and PUTing a resource with this field unset.
Constraint(s): The API server should allow POSTing and PUTing a resource with this field unset.
Interpretation: optionalフィールドの場合，APIサーバーはそのフィールドが未設定のPOST/PUTを許可すべきである．

Original: Some objects which are not persisted in the system - such as `SubjectAccessReview` and other webhook style calls - may choose to add `spec` and `status` to encapsulate a "call and response" pattern. The `spec` is the request (often a request for information) and the `status` is the response.
