For these RPC like objects the only operation may be POST, but having a consistent schema between submission and response reduces the complexity of these clients.
Constraint(s): Some objects which are not persisted in the system - such as `SubjectAccessReview` and other webhook style calls - may choose to add `spec` and `status` to encapsulate a "call and response" pattern.
Interpretation: SubjectAccessReviewのような非永続オブジェクトも，リクエスト/レスポンスパターンをカプセル化するためにspec/statusを使用してもよい．

Original: An optional field is one where the writer may choose to omit the field entirely. Readers should not assume that the field is present, unless the field also has a server-side default value.
Constraint(s): An optional field is one where the writer may choose to omit the field entirely.
Interpretation: optionalフィールドは書き手がフィールドを省略してよいフィールドである．

Original: * creationTimestamp: a string representing an RFC 3339 date of the date and time an object was created
Constraint(s): Every object SHOULD have a `metadata.creationTimestamp` field representing the RFC 3339 date and time at which the object was created.
Interpretation: すべてのオブジェクトはRFC 3339形式のmetadata.creationTimestampフィールドを持つべきである．

Original: Most objects defined in the system should have an endpoint that returns the full set of resources, as well as zero or more endpoints that return subsets of the full list. Some objects may be singletons (the current user, the system defaults) and may not have lists.
Constraint(s): Some objects may be singletons (the current user, the system defaults) and may not have lists.
Interpretation: シングルトンオブジェクト（例: 現在のユーザー，システムデフォルト）はリストエンドポイントを持たなくてもよい．

Original: * When asserting a behavioral requirement in the negative, use "may not". Examples: "may not be specified when otherField is empty", "only `name` may be specified".
Constraint(s): When asserting a behavioral requirement in the negative, use "may not". Examples: "may not be specified when otherField is empty", "only `name` may be specified".
Interpretation: エラーメッセージで否定的な動作要件を示す場合は「may not」を使用する（例: may not be specified when otherField is empty）．

Original: Fields must be either optional or required.
Constraint(s): Fields must be either optional or required.
Interpretation: フィールドはoptionalかrequiredのいずれかでなければならない．

Original: - Adding values to arrays which have mergeable semantics (`+listType=map` tag or `patchStrategy:"merge"` attribute in the type definition)
Constraint(s): All forms of defaulting should only add values to arrays with mergeable semantics (`+listType=map` tag or `patchStrategy:"merge"` attribute in the type definition).
Interpretation: すべてのデフォルト設定は，マージ可能なセマンティクス（+listType=mapタグ等）を持つ配列にのみ値を追加すべきである．

Original: * `fooDeadlineSeconds` is preferred for activity completion deadlines.
Constraint(s): `fooDeadlineSeconds` is preferred for activity completion deadlines.
Interpretation: アクティビティ完了のデッドラインにはfooDeadlineSecondsが望ましい．

Original: In particular we never want to change or override a value that was provided by the user.  If they requested something invalid, they should get an error.
Constraint(s): If they requested something invalid, they should get an error.
Interpretation: ユーザーが無効な値を要求した場合，デフォルト値で上書きするのではなく，エラーを返すべきである．

Original: `details` may contain extended data associated with the reason. Each reason may define its own extended details. This field is optional and the data returned is not guaranteed to conform to any schema except that defined by the reason type.
Constraint(s): `details` may contain extended data associated with the reason.
Interpretation: detailsフィールドはreasonに関連する拡張データを含んでもよい．

Original: Some resources in the v1 API contain fields called **`phase`**, and associated `message`, `reason`, and other status fields. The pattern of using `phase` is deprecated. Newer API types should use conditions instead. Phase was essentially a state-machine enumeration field, that contradicted [system-design principles](https://git.k8s.io/design-proposals-archive/architecture/principles.md#control-logic) and hampered evolution, since [adding new enum values breaks backward compatibility](api_changes.md). Rather than encouraging clients to infer implicit properties from phases, we prefer to explicitly expose the individual conditions that clients need to monitor. Conditions also have the benefit that it is possible to create some conditions with uniform meaning across all resource types, while still exposing others that are unique to specific resource types.  See [#7856](http://issues.k8s.io/7856) for more details and discussion.
Constraint(s): Newer API types should use conditions instead.
Interpretation: 新しいAPI型はphaseではなくconditionsを使用すべきである．

Original: * apiVersion: a string that identifies the version of the schema the object should have
Constraint(s): All JSON objects returned by an API MUST include an `apiVersion` field that identifies the version of the schema this object should have.
Interpretation: APIが返すすべてのJSONオブジェクトには，スキーマのバージョンを識別するapiVersionフィールドを含めなければならない．

Original: If an object reference may be used to reference resources of arbitrary types and the mapping between kind and resource could be ambiguous, "resource" should be used in the object reference.
Constraint(s): If an object reference may be used to reference resources of arbitrary types and the mapping between kind and resource could be ambiguous, "resource" should be used in the object reference.
Interpretation: 任意の型のリソースを参照でき，kindとresourceのマッピングが曖昧な場合，オブジェクト参照には「resource」を使用すべきである．

Original: Every simple kind returned by the server, and any simple kind sent to the server that must support idempotency or optimistic concurrency should return this value. Since simple resources are often used as input alternate actions that modify objects, the resource version of the simple resource should correspond to the resource version of the object.
Constraint(s): Every simple kind returned by the server, and any simple kind sent to the server that must support idempotency or optimistic concurrency should return this value.
Interpretation: サーバーが返すすべてのsimple kind，および冪等性や楽観的並行制御が必要なsimple kindはresourceVersionを返すべきである．

Original: A common set of "meta" API objects are used across all API groups and are thus considered part of the API group named `meta.k8s.io`. These types may evolve independent of the API group that uses them and API servers may allow them to be addressed in their generic form. Examples are `ListOptions`, `DeleteOptions`, `List`, `Status`, `WatchEvent`, and `Scale`. For historical reasons these types are part of each existing API group. Generic tools like quota, garbage collection, autoscalers, and generic clients like kubectl leverage these types to define consistent behavior across different resource types, like the interfaces in programming languages.
Constraint(s): These types may evolve independent of the API group that uses them and API servers may allow them to be addressed in their generic form.
Interpretation: 共通型はそれを使用するAPIグループとは独立して進化してもよく，汎用的な形式でアドレス可能にしてもよい．

Original: Objects may report multiple conditions, and new types of conditions may be added in the future or by 3rd party controllers. Therefore, conditions are represented using a list/slice of objects, where each condition has a similar structure. This collection should be treated as a map with a key of `type`.
Constraint(s): Objects may report multiple conditions, and new types of conditions may be added in the future or by 3rd party controllers.
Interpretation: オブジェクトは複数のconditionを報告でき，将来やサードパーティコントローラーが新しいcondition typeを追加してもよい．

Original: Names generated by the system may be requested using `metadata.generateName`. GenerateName indicates that the name should be made unique by the server prior to persisting it. A non-empty value for the field indicates the server should attempt to make the name unique (and the name returned to the client will be different than the name passed). The value of this field will be combined with a random suffix on the server if the Name field has not been provided. The provided value must be valid within the rules for Name, and may be truncated by the length of the suffix. If this field is specified, and Name is not present, the server will return a 409 with Reason `AlreadyExists` if the generated name exists, and the client should retry (after waiting at least the amount of time indicated in the Retry-After header, if it is present).
Constraint(s): A non-empty value for the field indicates the server should attempt to make the name unique (and the name returned to the client will be different than the name passed).
Interpretation: generateNameフィールドが非空の場合，サーバーは名前を一意にし，返却される名前は入力と異なるものになる．

Original: References to loosely coupled sets of objects, such as [pods](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/) overseen by a [replication controller](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/replicationcontroller/), are usually best referred to using a [label selector](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels/#label-selectors). In order to ensure that GETs of individual objects remain bounded in time and space, these sets may be queried via separate API queries, but will not be expanded in the referring object's status.
Constraint(s): In order to ensure that GETs of individual objects remain bounded in time and space, these sets may be queried via separate API queries, but will not be expanded in the referring object's status.
Interpretation: 関連オブジェクトの集合は別APIクエリで取得でき，参照元オブジェクトのstatus内に展開してはならない．個別GETの時間・空間を有界に保つため．

Original: Some APIs may need to identify which field in a JSON object is invalid, or to reference a value to extract from a separate resource. The current recommendation is to use standard JavaScript syntax for accessing that field, assuming the JSON object was transformed into a JavaScript object, without the leading dot, such as `metadata.name`.
Constraint(s): Some APIs may need to identify which field in a JSON object is invalid, or to reference a value to extract from a separate resource.
Interpretation: 一部のAPIは無効なフィールドの特定や別リソースからの値参照のため，JSONオブジェクト内のフィールド選択が必要になる場合がある．

Original: The resourceVersion is currently backed by [etcd's mod_revision](https://etcd.io/docs/latest/learning/api/#key-value-pair). However, it's important to note that the application should *not* rely on the implementation details of the versioning system maintained by Kubernetes. We may change the implementation of resourceVersion in the future, such as to change it to a timestamp or per-object counter.
Constraint(s): However, it's important to note that the application should *not* rely on the implementation details of the versioning system maintained by Kubernetes.
Interpretation: アプリケーションはKubernetesのバージョニングシステムの実装詳細に依存すべきではない．

Original: * Not all controllers will observe the previous advice about reporting "Unknown" or "False" values. For known conditions, the absence of a condition `status` should be interpreted the same as `Unknown`, and typically indicates that reconciliation has not yet finished (or that the resource state may not yet be observable).
Constraint(s): Not all controllers will observe the previous advice about reporting "Unknown" or "False" values. For known conditions, the absence of a condition `status` should be interpreted the same as `Unknown`, and typically indicates that reconciliation has not yet finished (or that the resource state may not yet be observable).
Interpretation: 既知のconditionが存在しない場合はUnknownと同じ意味に解釈すべきであり，通常はreconciliationが未完了であることを示す．

Original: Names generated by the system may be requested using `metadata.generateName`. GenerateName indicates that the name should be made unique by the server prior to persisting it. A non-empty value for the field indicates the server should attempt to make the name unique (and the name returned to the client will be different than the name passed). The value of this field will be combined with a random suffix on the server if the Name field has not been provided. The provided value must be valid within the rules for Name, and may be truncated by the length of the suffix. If this field is specified, and Name is not present, the server will return a 409 with Reason `AlreadyExists` if the generated name exists, and the client should retry (after waiting at least the amount of time indicated in the Retry-After header, if it is present).
Constraint(s): GenerateName indicates that the name should be made unique by the server prior to persisting it.
Interpretation: generateNameはサーバーが名前を一意にすべきことを示す．

Original: Inclusion of a system prefix in a label key is fairly hostile to UX. A prefix is only necessary in the case that the user cannot choose the label key, in order to avoid collisions with user-defined labels. However, I firmly believe that the user should always be allowed to select the label keys to use on their resources, so it should always be possible to override default label keys.
Constraint(s): However, I firmly believe that the user should always be allowed to select the label keys to use on their resources, so it should always be possible to override default label keys.
Interpretation: ユーザーは常にリソースに使用するラベルキーを選択でき，デフォルトのラベルキーをオーバーライドできるべきである．

Original: However, we should support conveniences for common cases by default. For example, what we now do in ReplicationController is automatically set the RC's selector and labels to the labels in the pod template by default, if they are not already set. That ensures that the selector will match the template, and that the RC can be managed using the same labels as the pods it creates. Note that once we generalize selectors, it won't necessarily be possible to unambiguously generate labels that match an arbitrary selector.
Constraint(s): However, we should support conveniences for common cases by default.
Interpretation: 一般的なケースに対してはデフォルトで便利な機能をサポートすべきである．

Original: When using this pattern, you must account for lifecycle of the allocated objects (who cleans them up and when) as well as the "linkage" between them and the main type (often using the same name, an object-ref field, or a selector).
Constraint(s): When using this pattern, you must account for lifecycle of the allocated objects (who cleans them up and when) as well as the "linkage" between them and the main type (often using the same name, an object-ref field, or a selector).
Interpretation: 別のオブジェクトとして割り当てるパターンを使用する場合，割り当てオブジェクトのライフサイクル（誰がいつ後始末するか）とメインの型との連携を考慮しなければならない．

Original: An object may have multiple resources that clients can use to perform specific actions that create, update, delete, or get.
Constraint(s): An object may have multiple resources that clients can use to perform specific actions that create, update, delete, or get.
Interpretation: 1つのオブジェクトに対して，CRUD操作のための複数のリソースエンドポイントを持つことが許容される．

Original: The term "kind" is reserved for these "top-level" API types. The term "type" should be used for distinguishing sub-categories within objects or subobjects.
Constraint(s): The term "type" should be used for distinguishing sub-categories within objects or subobjects.
Interpretation: オブジェクトやサブオブジェクト内のサブカテゴリの区別には「type」という用語を使用すべきである．

Original: `details` may contain extended data associated with the reason. Each reason may define its own extended details. This field is optional and the data returned is not guaranteed to conform to any schema except that defined by the reason type.
Constraint(s): Each reason may define its own extended details.
Interpretation: 各reasonは独自の拡張詳細を定義してもよい．

Original: In early versions of Kubernetes, some in-development features represented new API fields as annotations, generally with the form `something.alpha.kubernetes.io/name` or `something.beta.kubernetes.io/name` (depending on our confidence in it). This pattern is deprecated.  Some such annotations may still exist, but no new annotations may be defined.  New API fields are now developed as regular fields.
Constraint(s): This pattern is deprecated.
Interpretation: annotationsをAPI拡張フィールドとして使用するパターンは非推奨である．

Original: All map fields should be checked for maximum size.  If the map has a minimum size, that should also be checked.
Constraint(s): All map fields should be checked for maximum size.
Interpretation: すべてのmapフィールドは最大サイズのチェックを行うべきである．

Original: In the case of a list of object references, the field should be of the format "{field}Refs", with the same guidance as the singular case above.
Constraint(s): In the case of a list of object references, the field should be of the format "{field}Refs", with the same guidance as the singular case above.
Interpretation: オブジェクト参照のリストの場合，フィールド名は「{field}Refs」形式にすべきである．

Original: "Short names" listed in discovery may be used by clients as hints to resolve ambiguous user invocations to a single resource.
Constraint(s): "Short names" listed in discovery may be used by clients as hints to resolve ambiguous user invocations to a single resource.
Interpretation: ディスカバリに記載されたショートネームは，クライアントが曖昧なユーザー入力を単一リソースに解決するヒントとして使用可能．

Original: The name of the reference field should be of the format "{field}Ref", with "Ref" always included in the suffix.
Constraint(s): The name of the reference field should be of the format "{field}Ref", with "Ref" always included in the suffix.
Interpretation: 参照フィールドの名前は「{field}Ref」形式にすべきである（例: targetRef, secretRef）．

Original: Otherwise, PUT expects the whole object to be specified. Therefore, if a field is omitted it is assumed that the client wants to clear that field's value. The PUT verb does not accept partial updates. Modification of just part of an object may be achieved by GETting the resource, modifying part of the spec, labels, or annotations, and then PUTting it back. See [concurrency control](#concurrency-control-and-consistency), below, regarding read-modify-write consistency when using this pattern. Some objects may expose alternative resource representations that allow mutation of the status, or performing custom actions on the object.
Constraint(s): Some objects may expose alternative resource representations that allow mutation of the status, or performing custom actions on the object.
Interpretation: 一部のオブジェクトは，statusの変更やカスタムアクションの実行を可能にする代替リソース表現を公開してもよい．

Original: Duration fields must be represented as integer fields with units being part of the field name (e.g. `leaseDurationSeconds`). We don't use Duration in the API since that would require clients to implement go-compatible parsing.
Constraint(s): Duration fields must be represented as integer fields with units being part of the field name (e.g. `leaseDurationSeconds`).
Interpretation: 期間フィールドは整数フィールドとし，単位をフィールド名に含めなければならない（例: leaseDurationSeconds）．

Original: Almost all string fields should be checked for format.  Some format checks include checking for length (e.g. "short-name", aka, "DNS Label" must be less than 64 bytes).
Constraint(s): Some format checks include checking for length (e.g. "short-name", aka, "DNS Label" must be less than 64 bytes).
Interpretation: フォーマットチェックには長さチェックが含まれる（例: DNS Labelは64バイト未満でなければならない）．

Original: Some resources in the v1 API contain fields called **`phase`**, and associated `message`, `reason`, and other status fields. The pattern of using `phase` is deprecated. Newer API types should use conditions instead. Phase was essentially a state-machine enumeration field, that contradicted [system-design principles](https://git.k8s.io/design-proposals-archive/architecture/principles.md#control-logic) and hampered evolution, since [adding new enum values breaks backward compatibility](api_changes.md). Rather than encouraging clients to infer implicit properties from phases, we prefer to explicitly expose the individual conditions that clients need to monitor. Conditions also have the benefit that it is possible to create some conditions with uniform meaning across all resource types, while still exposing others that are unique to specific resource types.  See [#7856](http://issues.k8s.io/7856) for more details and discussion.
Constraint(s): The pattern of using `phase` is deprecated.
Interpretation: phaseフィールドの使用パターンは非推奨である．

Original: Controllers must take care to consider how a `status` field will be handled in the case of interrupted control loops (e.g. controller crash and restart), and must act idempotently and consistently.  This is particularly important when using an informer-fed cache, which might not be updated with recent writes. Using a resourceVersion precondition to detect the "conflict" is the common pattern in this case.  See [this issue](http://issue.k8s.io/105199) for an example.
Constraint(s): Controllers must take care to consider how a `status` field will be handled in the case of interrupted control loops (e.g. controller crash and restart), and must act idempotently and consistently.
Interpretation: コントローラーは制御ループの中断（クラッシュ・再起動）時のstatus処理を考慮し，冪等かつ一貫した動作をしなければならない．

Original: * When specifying inequalities, use words rather than symbols.  Examples: "must be less than 256", "must be greater than or equal to 0".  Do not use words like "larger than", "bigger than", "more than", "higher than", etc.
Constraint(s): When specifying inequalities, use words rather than symbols. Examples: "must be less than 256", "must be greater than or equal to 0". Do not use words like "larger than", "bigger than", "more than", "higher than", etc.
Interpretation: エラーメッセージで不等式を示す場合は記号ではなく単語を使用する（例: must be less than 256）．larger than等は使用しない．

Original: The `status` summarizes the current state of the object in the system, and is usually persisted with the object by automated processes but may be generated on the fly.  As a general guideline, fields in `status` should be the most recent observations of actual state, but they may contain information such as the results of allocations or similar operations which are executed in response to the object's `spec`.  See [below](#representing-allocated-values) for more details.
Constraint(s): The `status` summarizes the current state of the object in the system, and is usually persisted with the object by automated processes but may be generated on the fly.
Interpretation: statusはシステムにおけるオブジェクトの現在の状態を要約し，通常は永続化されるが，動的に生成されてもよい．

Original: New APIs should almost never do this.  Instead, they should use `status`. PersistentVolumes might have been simpler if we had done this.
Constraint(s): New APIs should almost never do this.
Interpretation: 新しいAPIでは，spec内に割り当て済み値を保持するパターンはほぼ使用すべきではない．

Original: Use of the `Reason` field is required.
Constraint(s): Use of the `Reason` field is required.
Interpretation: conditionのReasonフィールドの使用は必須である．

Original: Clients should use the SPDY protocols if their clients have native support, or WebSockets as a fallback. Note that WebSockets is susceptible to Head-of-Line blocking and so clients must read and process each message sequentially. In the future, an HTTP/2 implementation will be exposed that deprecates SPDY.
Constraint(s): Clients should use the SPDY protocols if their clients have native support, or WebSockets as a fallback.
Interpretation: クライアントはネイティブサポートがある場合SPDYプロトコルを使用し，フォールバックとしてWebSocketsを使用すべきである．

Original: Names generated by the system may be requested using `metadata.generateName`. GenerateName indicates that the name should be made unique by the server prior to persisting it. A non-empty value for the field indicates the server should attempt to make the name unique (and the name returned to the client will be different than the name passed). The value of this field will be combined with a random suffix on the server if the Name field has not been provided. The provided value must be valid within the rules for Name, and may be truncated by the length of the suffix. If this field is specified, and Name is not present, the server will return a 409 with Reason `AlreadyExists` if the generated name exists, and the client should retry (after waiting at least the amount of time indicated in the Retry-After header, if it is present).
Constraint(s): The provided value must be valid within the rules for Name, and may be truncated by the length of the suffix.
Interpretation: generateNameの値はName規則内で有効でなければならず，サフィックスの長さにより切り詰められることがある．

Original: * `/status`: Used to write just the `status` portion of a resource. For example, the `/pods` endpoint only allows updates to `metadata` and `spec`, since those reflect end-user intent. An automated process should be able to modify status for users to see by sending an updated Pod kind to the server to the "/pods/<name>/status" endpoint - the alternate endpoint allows different rules to be applied to the update, and access to be appropriately restricted.
Constraint(s): `/status`: Used to write just the `status` portion of a resource. For example, the `/pods` endpoint only allows updates to `metadata` and `spec`, since those reflect end-user intent. An automated process should be able to modify status for users to see by sending an updated Pod kind to the server to the "/pods/<name>/status" endpoint - the alternate endpoint allows different rules to be applied to the update, and access to be appropriately restricted.
Interpretation: /statusサブリソースは，statusのみの更新に使用される．異なるアクセス制御ルールを適用でき，自動プロセスがステータスを更新する際に利用する．

Original: The goal is that, for a given input (e.g. YAML file), PUT on an existing object should produce the same result as if you used that input to create the object. Calling PUT a second time with the same input should be idempotent and should not change the resource.  Even a read-modify-write cycle is not a perfect solution in the face of version skew.
Constraint(s): The goal is that, for a given input (e.g. YAML file), PUT on an existing object should produce the same result as if you used that input to create the object.
Interpretation: 同じ入力に対してPUT（既存オブジェクト更新）とCREATE（新規作成）は同じ結果を生成すべきである．

Original: Condition `status` values may be `True`, `False`, or `Unknown`. The absence of a condition should be interpreted the same as `Unknown`.  How controllers handle `Unknown` depends on the Condition in question.
Constraint(s): The absence of a condition should be interpreted the same as `Unknown`.
Interpretation: conditionが存在しない場合はUnknownと同じ意味に解釈すべきである．

Original: * The server should set the `Retry-After` HTTP header and return `retryAfterSeconds` in the details field of the object. A value of `0` is the default.
Constraint(s): The server should set the `Retry-After` HTTP header and return `retryAfterSeconds` in the details field of the object. A value of `0` is the default.
Interpretation: サーバーはRetry-After HTTPヘッダーを設定し，detailsフィールドにretryAfterSecondsを返すべきである．デフォルトは0．

Original: Note that for backward compatibility, any field that has the `omitempty` struct tag, and is not explicitly marked as `+required`, will be considered to be optional. This is expected to change in the future, and new fields should explicitly set either an `+optional` or `+required` comment tag.
Constraint(s): New fields should explicitly set either an `+optional` or `+required` comment tag.
Interpretation: 新規フィールドには+optionalまたは+requiredのコメントタグを明示的に設定すべきである．

Original: Resource implementers can optionally include "short names" and categories in the discovery information published for a resource type, which clients may use as hints when resolving ambiguous user invocations.
Constraint(s): Resource implementers can optionally include "short names" and categories in the discovery information published for a resource type, which clients may use as hints when resolving ambiguous user invocations.
Interpretation: リソース実装者はディスカバリ情報にショートネームやカテゴリを含めることができ，クライアントが曖昧なユーザー入力を解決するヒントとして利用可能．

Original: * DELETE /<resourceNamePlural> - Deletes a list of type <resourceName>, e.g. DELETE /pods a list of Pods.
Constraint(s): API resources should use the traditional REST pattern: DELETE /<resourceNamePlural> - Deletes a list of type <resourceName>, e.g. DELETE /pods a list of Pods.
Interpretation: DELETE /<リソース名複数形>でリスト削除エンドポイントを提供すべきである．

Original: The common theme among all of these is that the system should not trust users with such fields, and must verify or otherwise confirm such requests before using them.
Constraint(s): The common theme among all of these is that the system should not trust users with such fields, and must verify or otherwise confirm such requests before using them.
Interpretation: システムはユーザーが指定するフィールドを信頼すべきではなく，使用前にそれらの要求を検証・確認しなければならない．

Original: Object references on a namespaced type should usually refer only to objects in the same namespace.  Because namespaces are a security boundary, cross namespace references can have unexpected impacts, including:
Constraint(s): Object references on a namespaced type should usually refer only to objects in the same namespace.
Interpretation: namespace付きリソースのオブジェクト参照は通常，同じnamespace内のオブジェクトのみを参照すべきである．

Original: All fields should be declared optional or required, and validation should check the requiredness.
Constraint(s): All fields should be declared optional or required, and validation should check the requiredness.
Interpretation: すべてのフィールドはoptionalまたはrequiredとして宣言し，バリデーションで必須性をチェックすべきである．

Original: Many simple resources are "subresources", which are rooted at API paths of specific resources. When resources wish to expose alternative actions or views that are closely coupled to a single resource, they should do so using new sub-resources. Common subresources include:
Constraint(s): When resources wish to expose alternative actions or views that are closely coupled to a single resource, they should do so using new sub-resources.
Interpretation: 単一リソースに密結合した代替アクションやビューは，サブリソースとして公開すべきである（例: /pods/{name}/log）．

Original: Condition types should be named in PascalCase. Short condition names are preferred (e.g. "Ready" over "MyResourceReady").
Constraint(s): Short condition names are preferred (e.g. "Ready" over "MyResourceReady").
Interpretation: 短いcondition名が望ましい（例: MyResourceReadyよりReadyが好ましい）．

Original: Like admission controlled defaults, these controllers may consider external state when deciding what values to set, must handle race conditions, and can be disabled.  Fields which are initialized this way must be strictly optional (meaning observers will see the object without these fields set, and that is allowable and semantically correct).
Constraint(s): Like admission controlled defaults, these controllers may consider external state when deciding what values to set, must handle race conditions, and can be disabled.
Interpretation: 遅延初期化コントローラーは外部状態を考慮でき，レースコンディションに対応し，無効化可能でなければならない．

Original: Some fields will have a list of allowed values (enumerations). These values will be strings, and they will be in CamelCase, with an initial uppercase letter. Examples: `ClusterFirst`, `Pending`, `ClientIP`. When an acronym or initialism each letter in the acronym should be uppercase, such as with `ClientIP` or `TCPDelay`. When a proper name or the name of a command-line executable is used as a constant the proper name should be represented in consistent casing - examples: `systemd`, `iptables`, `IPVS`, `cgroupfs`, `Docker` (as a generic concept), `docker` (as the command-line executable). If a proper name is used which has mixed capitalization like `eBPF` that should be preserved in a longer constant such as `eBPFDelegation`.
Constraint(s): When a proper name or the name of a command-line executable is used as a constant the proper name should be represented in consistent casing - examples: `systemd`, `iptables`, `IPVS`, `cgroupfs`, `Docker` (as a generic concept), `docker` (as the command-line executable).
Interpretation: 固有名詞やコマンドライン実行ファイル名を定数として使う場合，一貫したケーシングで表現すべきである（例: systemd, iptables, IPVS, Docker）．

Original: In condition types, and everywhere else they appear in the API, **`Reason`** is intended to be a one-word, CamelCase representation of the category of cause of the current status, and **`Message`** is intended to be a human-readable phrase or sentence, which may contain specific details of the individual occurrence. `Reason` is intended to be used in concise output, such as one-line `kubectl get` output, and in summarizing occurrences of causes, whereas `Message` is intended to be presented to users in detailed status explanations, such as `kubectl describe` output.
Constraint(s): In condition types, and everywhere else they appear in the API, `Reason` is intended to be a one-word, CamelCase representation of the category of cause of the current status, and `Message` is intended to be a human-readable phrase or sentence, which may contain specific details of the individual occurrence.
Interpretation: Reasonは原因カテゴリの1ワードCamelCase表現，Messageは個別事象の人間可読な文またはフレーズとして使用する．

Original: When the user chooses to change the `discriminator` value (e.g., from `Type X` to `Type Y`) without changing any other fields then the system should clear the fields that were used to represent `Type X` in the union along with releasing resources that were attached to `Type X`. This should automatically happen irrespective of how these values and resources were allocated (i.e., reserved by the user or automatically allocated by the system. A concrete example of this is again `Service` API. The system allocates resources such as `NodePorts` and `ClusterIPs` and automatically fill in the fields that represent them in case of the service is of type `NodePort` or `ClusterIP` (`discriminator` values). These resources and the fields representing them are automatically cleared when  the users changes service type to `ExternalName` where these resources and field values no longer apply.
Constraint(s): This should automatically happen irrespective of how these values and resources were allocated (i.e., reserved by the user or automatically allocated by the system.
Interpretation: 値やリソースの割り当て方法（ユーザー指定/システム自動）にかかわらず，自動的にクリアが行われるべきである．

Original: The name of a list kind must end with "List". Lists have a limited set of common metadata. All lists use the required "items" field to contain the array of objects they return. Any kind that has the "items" field must be a list kind.
Constraint(s): All lists use the required "items" field to contain the array of objects they return.
Interpretation: すべてのリスト型は，返却するオブジェクト配列を格納するための「items」フィールドを必須で持つ．

Original: An optional field is one where the writer may choose to omit the field entirely. Readers should not assume that the field is present, unless the field also has a server-side default value.
Constraint(s): Readers should not assume that the field is present, unless the field also has a server-side default value.
Interpretation: 読み手はフィールドの存在を前提としてはならない（サーバーサイドデフォルト値がある場合を除く）．

Original: All API within Kubernetes must leverage constants in this style, including flags and configuration files. Where inconsistent constants were previously used, new flags should be CamelCase only, and over time old flags should be updated to accept a CamelCase value alongside the inconsistent constant. Example: the Kubelet accepts a `--topology-manager-policy` flag that has values `none`, `best-effort`, `restricted`, and `single-numa-node`. This flag should accept `None`, `BestEffort`, `Restricted`, and `SingleNUMANode` going forward. If new values are added to the flag, both forms should be supported.
Constraint(s): If new values are added to the flag, both forms should be supported.
Interpretation: フラグに新しい値を追加する場合，CamelCaseと既存形式の両方をサポートすべきである．

Original: Almost all string fields should be checked for format.  Some format checks include checking for length (e.g. "short-name", aka, "DNS Label" must be less than 64 bytes).
Constraint(s): Almost all string fields should be checked for format.
Interpretation: ほぼすべての文字列フィールドはフォーマットチェックを行うべきである．

Original: Like all controllers, care must be taken to not clobber unrelated fields or values (e.g. in an array).  Using one of the patch or apply mechanisms is recommended to facilitate composition and concurrency of controllers.
Constraint(s): Using one of the patch or apply mechanisms is recommended to facilitate composition and concurrency of controllers.
Interpretation: コントローラーの合成と並行性を促進するために，patchまたはapplyメカニズムの使用が推奨される．

Original: * Acronyms should similarly only be used when extremely commonly known. All letters in the acronym should have the same case, using the appropriate case for the situation. For example, at the beginning of a field name, the acronym should be all lowercase, such as "httpGet". Where used as a constant, all letters should be uppercase, such as "TCP" or "UDP".
Constraint(s): Acronyms should similarly only be used when extremely commonly known. All letters in the acronym should have the same case, using the appropriate case for the situation. For example, at the beginning of a field name, the acronym should be all lowercase, such as "httpGet". Where used as a constant, all letters should be uppercase, such as "TCP" or "UDP".
Interpretation: 頭字語は広く知られている場合のみ使用し，全文字を同じケースにすべきである（フィールド先頭: httpGet，定数: TCP, UDP）．

Original: - The API server should not allow POSTing or PUTing a resource with this field unset.
Constraint(s): The API server should not allow POSTing or PUTing a resource with this field unset.
Interpretation: requiredフィールドの場合，APIサーバーはそのフィールドが未設定のPOST/PUTを拒否すべきである．

Original: - Key names, including prefixes, should be precise enough that a user could plausibly understand where it came from and what it is for.
Constraint(s): Key names, including prefixes, should be precise enough that a user could plausibly understand where it came from and what it is for.
Interpretation: キー名はプレフィックスを含め，出自と目的をユーザーが理解できるよう十分に正確であるべきである．

Original: APIs may return alternative representations of any resource in response to an Accept header or under alternative endpoints, but the default serialization for input and output of API responses MUST be JSON.
Constraint(s): APIs may return alternative representations of any resource in response to an Accept header or under alternative endpoints, but the default serialization for input and output of API responses MUST be JSON.
Interpretation: APIレスポンスのデフォルトシリアライゼーションはJSONでなければならない．Acceptヘッダーに応じた代替表現は許容される．

Original: * `FooController` is a deprecated kind naming convention. Name the kind after the thing being controlled instead (e.g., `Job` rather than `JobController`).
Constraint(s): `FooController` is a deprecated kind naming convention. Name the kind after the thing being controlled instead (e.g., `Job` rather than `JobController`).
Interpretation: FooControllerは非推奨のkind命名規約である．制御対象にちなんで命名すべきである（例: JobControllerではなくJob）．

Original: In early versions of Kubernetes, some in-development features represented new API fields as annotations, generally with the form `something.alpha.kubernetes.io/name` or `something.beta.kubernetes.io/name` (depending on our confidence in it). This pattern is deprecated.  Some such annotations may still exist, but no new annotations may be defined.  New API fields are now developed as regular fields.
Constraint(s): Some such annotations may still exist, but no new annotations may be defined.
Interpretation: 既存のそのようなannotationsは残存しうるが，新規のannotationsは定義できない．

Original: Built-in types and ownerReferences do not support cross namespaces references. If a non-built-in types chooses to have cross-namespace references the semantics of the edge cases above should be clearly described and the permissions issues should be resolved. This could be done with a double opt-in (an opt-in from both the referrer and the refer-ee) or with secondary permissions checks performed in admission.
Constraint(s): If a non-built-in types chooses to have cross-namespace references the semantics of the edge cases above should be clearly described and the permissions issues should be resolved.
Interpretation: 非組み込み型がクロスnamespace参照を持つ場合，エッジケースのセマンティクスを明確に説明し，権限の問題を解決すべきである．

Original: Names generated by the system may be requested using `metadata.generateName`. GenerateName indicates that the name should be made unique by the server prior to persisting it. A non-empty value for the field indicates the server should attempt to make the name unique (and the name returned to the client will be different than the name passed). The value of this field will be combined with a random suffix on the server if the Name field has not been provided. The provided value must be valid within the rules for Name, and may be truncated by the length of the suffix. If this field is specified, and Name is not present, the server will return a 409 with Reason `AlreadyExists` if the generated name exists, and the client should retry (after waiting at least the amount of time indicated in the Retry-After header, if it is present).
Constraint(s): Names generated by the system may be requested using `metadata.generateName`.
Interpretation: システム生成の名前はmetadata.generateNameで要求できる．

Original: All numeric fields should be bounds-checked, both for too-small values (including negative) and for too-large values.
Constraint(s): All numeric fields should be bounds-checked, both for too-small values (including negative) and for too-large values.
Interpretation: すべての数値フィールドは，小さすぎる値（負の値を含む）と大きすぎる値の両方について境界チェックすべきである．

Original: Since almost everything is happening asynchronously to almost everything else, controller implementations should take care around the ordering of operations. For example, whether the controller updates a `status` field before or after it actuates a change depends on what guarantees need to be made to observers of the system.  In some cases, writing to a `status` field represents an acknowledgement or acceptance of a `spec` value, and it is OK to write it before actuation.  However, if it would be problematic for a client to observe the `status` value before it is actuated then the controller must actuate first and update `status` afterward.  In some rarer cases, controllers will need to acknowledge, then actuate, then update to a "final" value.
Constraint(s): Since almost everything is happening asynchronously to almost everything else, controller implementations should take care around the ordering of operations.
Interpretation: ほぼすべてが非同期で動作するため，コントローラーの実装は操作の順序に注意すべきである．

Original: * name: a string that uniquely identifies this object within the current namespace (see [the identifiers docs](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/)). This value is used in the path when retrieving an individual object.
Constraint(s): Every object kind MUST have the following metadata in a nested object field called "metadata": name: a string that uniquely identifies this object within the current namespace (see the identifiers docs). This value is used in the path when retrieving an individual object.
Interpretation: すべてのオブジェクトkindは，現在のnamespace内で一意なmetadata.nameフィールドを持たなければならない．

Original: Objects that contain both `spec` and `status` should not contain additional top-level fields other than the standard metadata fields.
Constraint(s): Objects that contain both `spec` and `status` should not contain additional top-level fields other than the standard metadata fields.
Interpretation: specとstatusの両方を持つオブジェクトは，標準のmetadataフィールド以外のトップレベルフィールドを持つべきではない．

Original: All list fields should be checked for maximum size.  If the list has a minimum size, that should also be checked.
Constraint(s): All list fields should be checked for maximum size.
Interpretation: すべてのリストフィールドは最大サイズのチェックを行うべきである．

Original: References in the `status` of the referee to the referrer may be permitted, when the references are one-to-one and do not need to be frequently updated, particularly in an edge-based manner.
Constraint(s): References in the `status` of the referee to the referrer may be permitted, when the references are one-to-one and do not need to be frequently updated, particularly in an edge-based manner.
Interpretation: 被参照側のstatusから参照側への参照は，1対1でかつ頻繁な更新が不要な場合に許容される．

Original: * GET /<resourceNamePlural>?watch=true - Receive a stream of JSON objects corresponding to changes made to any resource of the given kind over time.
Constraint(s): API resources should use the traditional REST pattern: GET /<resourceNamePlural>?watch=true - Receive a stream of JSON objects corresponding to changes made to any resource of the given kind over time.
Interpretation: GET /<リソース名複数形>?watch=trueで，リソースの変更に対応するJSONオブジェクトのストリームを受信するエンドポイントを提供すべきである．

Original: Units must either be explicit in the field name (e.g., `timeoutSeconds`), or must be specified as part of the value (e.g., `resource.Quantity`). Which approach is preferred is TBD, though currently we use the `fooSeconds` convention for durations.
Constraint(s): Which approach is preferred is TBD, though currently we use the `fooSeconds` convention for durations.
Interpretation: 単位の表現方法は未確定だが，現在は期間に対してfooSeconds規約を使用している．

Original: * labels: a map of string keys and values that can be used to organize and categorize objects (see [the labels docs](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels/))
Constraint(s): Every object SHOULD have a `metadata.labels` field containing string key-value pairs used to organize and categorize objects.
Interpretation: すべてのオブジェクトは，オブジェクトの整理・分類に使用する文字列key-valueペアのmetadata.labelsフィールドを持つべきである．

Original: `reason` may contain a machine-readable, one-word, CamelCase description of why this operation is in the `Failure` status. If this value is empty there is no information available. The `reason` clarifies an HTTP status code but does not override it.
Constraint(s): `reason` may contain a machine-readable, one-word, CamelCase description of why this operation is in the `Failure` status.
Interpretation: reasonフィールドは機械可読な1ワードCamelCaseの失敗理由を含んでもよい．

Original: * resourceVersion: a string that identifies the internal version of this object that can be used by clients to determine when objects have changed. This value MUST be treated as opaque by clients and passed unmodified back to the server. Clients should not assume that the resource version has meaning across namespaces, different kinds of resources, or different servers. (See [concurrency control](#concurrency-control-and-consistency), below, for more details.)
Constraint(s): Every object SHOULD have the following metadata in a nested object field called "metadata": resourceVersion: a string that identifies the internal version of this object that can be used by clients to determine when objects have changed. This value MUST be treated as opaque by clients and passed unmodified back to the server. Clients should not assume that the resource version has meaning across namespaces, different kinds of resources, or different servers. (See concurrency control, below, for more details.)
Interpretation: すべてのオブジェクトはmetadata.resourceVersionを持つべきである．この値はクライアントが不透明に扱い，変更せずにサーバーに返さなければならない（MUST）．
